segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a brightly lit 'TOP TALENT' stage, featuring dynamic blue and purple lighting, a large, engaged audience, and a judging panel seated behind a desk with prominent 'X' logos. Adrian Stoica, a bald man with a beard wearing a light blue t-shirt and patterned pajama pants, stands center stage holding a microphone. He greets the audience with a cheerful "Hi!" and "Hello," which is met with enthusiastic applause and cheers. A male judge welcomes him to AGT, prompting Adrian to introduce himself as "Adrian" from "Italy," eliciting further cheers and applause from the visibly engaged audience. The judge then inquires about pets, to which Adrian proudly introduces his "best friend," Hurricane, a calm black dog sitting attentively on stage, explaining they met nine years ago. Adrian reveals his profession as a "dog coach" when asked about his day job. The judge asks what he'll be doing tonight, and Adrian states he will "play with Hurricane." The stage is set with playful props including a small dog bed with paw prints, a yellow armchair with a green bone emblem, a side table with a clock, and a coat rack, hinting at a comedic or pet-related act. The judges, including a woman in a red dress and a man in a dark shirt, react with amusement, smiles, and laughter throughout the interaction. As on-screen graphics confirm the performers as 'ADRIAN STOICA & HURRICANE', the judge wishes Adrian good luck, and the segment concludes with a burst of audience applause and music, setting the stage for their performance.

The man’s casual attire and the whimsical stage design contribute to a lighthearted, humorous tone.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of the Judging Panel, where the man in the dark shirt points toward the stage, and the woman in the red dress reacts with surprise, as the audience erupts in cheers and applause, with a woman exclaiming "Oh!" and another adding "Oh, my God, so cute." The scene transitions to a wide shot of the stage, revealing Adrian Stoica and Hurricane, his black dog, performing a comedic routine amidst a lighthearted, homey set featuring a small bed with paw-print bedding and a yellow armchair with a green bone emblem. Adrian, dressed in a light blue t-shirt and patterned pajama pants, is initially seen lying in the bed, while Hurricane sits calmly on the armchair. As the act progresses, Adrian dramatically wakes up, throwing off the blanket with a distinct "boing" sound effect, and begins a playful chase with Hurricane across the stage. The dog leaps onto the armchair, then runs around Adrian, who pretends to be startled and falls to the ground, prompting further laughter and applause from the visibly amused judges and audience, with the woman in the red dress clapping enthusiastically. Upbeat pop music with a female vocalist begins, singing lyrics like "Don't even think about it, just move your body," "Listen to the music, sing for it all," "Just move those legs, go ahead, get crazy," "Anyone can do it, sing for it all," "Show the world you've got that fire," "Feel the rhythm, get it louder," "Show them what you can do, prove to them you've got the moves," and "I don't know about you, but I feel better when I'm dancing." The performance culminates with Adrian rolling on the floor as Hurricane circles him, maintaining the comedic energy. On-screen graphics display 'TOP TALENT' and the performers' names 'ADRIAN STOICA & HURRICANE' in vibrant blue and pink text, all under bright theatrical lighting with dynamic blue and purple hues.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Adrian Stoica lying on a brightly lit stage, featuring a vibrant cityscape-themed backdrop and the 'America's Got Talent' logo, playfully interacting with his dog Hurricane. Upbeat electronic dance music with female vocals, singing phrases like "Yeah, yeah, better when I'm dancing," sets an energetic tone as Hurricane, a black dog, leaps onto Adrian's back as he rolls. Adrian, dressed in a light blue t-shirt and patterned pajama pants, performs a comedic routine where Hurricane actively participates, jumping, running, and circling him. Adrian then stands and moves to a clothing rack, dramatically removing a beige garment while Hurricane circles. He proceeds to put on a hat and coat, mimicking a morning routine, as Hurricane continues to follow and interact, all in sync with the lively music. The act includes Adrian rolling on the floor again, with Hurricane jumping over him, and then standing up to perform a playful dance with the dog. The audience and judging panel, including a woman in a red dress, are visibly amused, clapping and laughing enthusiastically, their cheers and applause clearly audible and punctuating the music. Stage props—a dog bed with paw prints, a yellow armchair with a green bone emblem, a side table with a clock, a coat rack, and a yellow pet carrier—remain consistent, enhancing the domestic comedy theme. On-screen graphics display the show's logo and the performers' names 'ADRIAN STOICA & HURRICANE' in a stylized blue and pink font. The performance concludes with Adrian and Hurricane engaging in a final playful leap over the armchair, drawing loud cheers and applause from the audience as the music continues its energetic beat.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, rhythmic musical track as Adrian Stoica, a bald man with a beard in a light blue t-shirt and patterned pajama pants, performs a comedic routine with his black dog, Hurricane, on a stage set with domestic props like a yellow armchair and a dog bed. Adrian playfully interacts with Hurricane, who leaps and runs around him with high energy, while Adrian sings a comedic, slightly off-key rendition of 'I feel better when I'm dancing' and exclaims 'Hello!' and 'He wants the TV!' as Hurricane jumps onto the yellow armchair. The audience, seated in tiered rows, reacts with loud laughter and applause, while the Judging Panel, consisting of three judges at a desk with 'X' logos, shows visible amusement. The woman in the red dress claps and laughs, exclaiming 'I need a dog like that,' and the man in the dark shirt points and reacts with amusement, adding 'I need a dog like that, too,' and 'Oh my god!' On-screen graphics display 'America's Got Talent' and 'ADRIAN STOICA & HURRICANE'. A female voice, likely one of the judges, is heard saying 'You remember them!' and then recounts, 'In London, our first audition, I came with two dogs,' to which Adrian interjects, 'Bonnie and Simba,' and the female voice confirms, 'And Simba, yeah. We did a dance.' Adrian then comically adds, 'We didn't think Simba was very good,' which is immediately followed by a loud, sustained buzzer sound, eliciting gasps from the audience. The performance culminates with Hurricane leaping off the armchair and running toward the judges' desk, prompting the judges to stand and applaud. The scene transitions to a new act as a woman in a blue blazer and hat, accompanied by a calm brown and white dog, walks onto the stage, with the stage lighting shifting to vibrant blues and purples, and the audience continuing to clap and cheer. The video ends with the judges seated at their desk, the red 'X' lights illuminated, as the new act begins.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the brightly lit 'Britain's Got Talent' stage, featuring its prominent logo, as four judges are seated at their 'X' marked desk and the audience is illuminated by blue and red stage lights. A woman in a blue blazer and wide-brimmed hat walks onto the stage with a calm, well-trained brown and white dog. A male judge's voice asks if 'Bonnie' will play an instrument this time, to which the woman confirms, 'Yep. She will play piano.' The judge exclaims 'Seriously?' and then 'Unbelievable!' as the dog, with long floppy ears and a fluffy coat, is led to a grand piano, where it stands on its hind legs, seemingly interacting with the keys. The judges react with amusement and surprise, with the woman in the red dress pointing and laughing, and a male judge gesturing animatedly, while another male judge declares, 'This is something the world's never seen before.' The audience erupts in cheers and applause. The first male judge exclaims 'Oh my gosh!' and adds, 'You thought Susan Boyle was big, you wait till this goes viral,' before wishing them 'Good luck,' to which the woman replies, 'Thank you.' Dramatic, building orchestral music begins as the dog runs across the stage, leaping over a small barrier. The woman in the blue blazer follows, guiding the dog through a series of energetic and precise tricks, including standing on its hind legs and moving around the piano. The judges continue to react with delight and astonishment, with the male judge leaning forward and exclaiming 'Oh my gosh!' again amidst the triumphant music. The stage is adorned with vibrant blue and purple lighting and dynamic geometric patterns. The dog's performance draws enthusiastic cheers and applause from both the judges and the audience, culminating with the dog standing proudly on the piano as the music swells and the crowd applauds loudly.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with upbeat, orchestral music as a judge, a man with short gray hair in a black quilted jacket, reacts with surprise. On stage, a woman in a blue blazer and black pants, identified by on-screen text as 'OLGA, AGED 45', guides a brown and white dog with long floppy ears, named 'BONNIE, AGED 4', beside a grand piano. Bonnie, wearing a white collar, performs tricks, including standing on its hind legs and interacting with the piano, producing distinct jazzy sounds. A male judge exclaims with surprise and excitement, "It's playing the piano!" and "It's playing jazz!" The audience is visibly engaged, with audible clapping, laughter, and cheers. Thank you so much!" accompanied by celebratory music and continued audience applause.

The dog performs various tricks, including standing on its hind legs and interacting with a grand piano, while the woman gestures with a hat and a stick. The stage features dynamic geometric patterns and the 'Britain's Got Talent Logo'. The audience is visibly engaged, clapping along to the music. The camera cuts to the judging panel, showing three judges: a man with short gray hair in a black quilted jacket reacting with surprise, a woman in a red dress with large gold hoop earrings smiling and clapping, and the man in the black shirt, all reacting with smiles and applause. As the performance concludes with the woman raising her arms in triumph, the music swells, and the man in the black shirt (Simon Cowell) speaks into a microphone, declaring, "Sorry Scooby Doo, sorry Snoopy, there is a new famous dog in town." A female judge (Alesha Dixon) then congratulates him, saying, "Well done Simon, you gave that an ovation!" Simon responds, explaining, "Well, I did, because this is the first time genuinely a dog has played an instrument on one of our shows." When asked about the song, another judge (Bruno Tonioli) interjects, "Jazz!" which Simon confirms. Simon then humorously adds, "I do feel bad for Simba, but Simba was holding Bonnie back," before concluding, "I think that was unbelievable." The audience continues to cheer and clap enthusiastically as the celebratory music plays.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with close-ups of the Judging Panel, where the woman in a red dress with large gold hoop earrings (Amanda Holden) laughs heartily, while the man with short gray hair in a black quilted jacket (Simon Cowell) reacts with surprise and amusement. The man with a beard in a black shirt (Bruno Tonioli) gestures animatedly, humorously comparing the dog's sounds to horror movie soundtracks, stating, "I think you know this kind of soundtracks for horror movies..." and later adding, "That's never happened before." The camera then cuts to the Audience, who are visibly entertained, clapping, cheering, and smiling amidst background music. The scene shifts to the stage, where the Woman in Blue Blazer and Hat (Olga) stands confidently with the Brown and White Dog (Nymeria), which is wearing a small black bow tie. The dog performs a series of tricks, including standing on its hind legs and interacting with a Grand Piano, guided by Olga using a stick. The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lighting, and the backdrop features dynamic geometric patterns. Olga raises her arms in triumph as the dog completes its routine. The Judging Panel is shown again, with all three judges reacting positively—clapping and smiling. Amanda Holden praises the act, saying, "I think she's amazing. She can do it all. And writes her own songs! I just love you as well, Olga. You're both fabulous. Thank you, thank you!" The judges then cast their votes, with Amanda declaring, "I'm gonna start by saying yes. Yay!", followed by another female judge (Alesha Dixon) saying, "I'm saying yes." Simon Cowell adds, "Obviously, yes!", and Bruno Tonioli concludes with, "Okay, yes!" The camera pans to the Audience, who are cheering and laughing loudly. The video concludes with a close-up of Olga, now holding Nymeria, with the Britain's Got Talent Logo prominently displayed in the background. On-Screen Graphics appear, displaying the performers' names 'AMBER AND NYMERIA' in a stylized graphic, accompanied by a Union Jack flag and a glowing star, as Olga's voice is heard expressing nervousness, "I'm really nervous, like, it's now or never." The overall atmosphere is lively and celebratory, filled with audience applause, cheers, and background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the vibrant Britain's Got Talent stage, featuring a geometrically patterned backdrop illuminated with dynamic blue and purple lights, and the iconic 'BRITAIN'S GOT TALENT' logo. A woman in a black sequined outfit and top hat, later identified as Amber, instructs her white and gray speckled dog, Nameria, a two-year-old Border Collie, with a clear "Ready, sit." The Judging Panel, consisting of three judges seated at their desk with red buzzers, reacts with surprise and amusement. The woman in the pink shiny dress greets them with an enthusiastic "Oh, hello," and asks, "Who are you?" Amber responds, "I'm Amber," and introduces her dog, "This is Nameria," prompting audience applause and a warm "Hello Nameria" from the judge, as Nameria makes soft panting sounds. The judge inquires about the dog's breed, to which Amber confirms, "Uh, she's a Border Collie," and the judge praises her "gorgeous coloring." Amber, revealing they are "from Holland," shares her emotional journey, stating, "it's always been our biggest dream to achieve something like this and now standing here, I'm almost crying," expressing gratitude with "So I'm so thankful to be here. Thank you." The judge also learns Nameria is "two years old" and that the routine took "seven or eight months" of "mostly every day" rehearsal. The judge offers encouragement, "Okay, this next two minutes could change your life. Good luck," to which Amber replies, "Thank you." Amber then guides Nameria with a cane, instructing "Ready, go, ready, just," and an emphatic "Yes!" as the dog performs tricks like sitting, standing on hind legs, and walking across the stage, engaging the audience who clap and laugh. Nameria precisely places her paw on a red cup on a small table, followed by Amber's encouraging "Sit. Good girl. Play, ready, super." The dog then jumps up to lick Amber’s face in a moment of affection. The stage lighting remains bright and colorful, enhancing the energetic atmosphere. The Britain's Got Talent logo is shown again as Amber and Nameria conclude their act with a final pose. The judges react with delight, particularly the woman in the pink dress, who laughs and claps enthusiastically, while the man with a beard in a black shirt remarks, "Here we go." The dog’s performance, marked by precision and charm, captivates the audience throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The Britain's Got Talent stage glows with its illuminated logo and vibrant blue and purple lighting as the judging panel, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, watches intently. The Woman in Black Sequined Outfit and Top Hat, affectionately cradling her calm White and Gray Speckled Dog, receives enthusiastic praise. Amanda Holden, in her pink shiny dress, exclaims, "That was incredible to watch. I was speechless," adding, "you've done an incredible job." The woman, holding her dog in its black vest with a gold star, responds with a grateful "Thank you so much." A slow, emotional background music track with a female vocalist begins as Simon Cowell, speaking into his microphone and gesturing, observes the "love" and "chemistry" between them, calling it "wonderful to see." He praises both, stating, "It's not just a talented dog, it's a very talented owner too," and declares their chemistry "absolutely fantastic." David Walliams, in his brown suit, then chimes in, expressing his love for "dog acts" and proclaiming this one "in a different league," the "best dog act we've had since Ashley and Pudsey." The woman again thanks him, "Wow, thank you so much." Simon then lightens the mood with a humorous anecdote about collies evolving to become like dentists, prompting Amanda to playfully ask, "Where was he reading that?" The audience's clapping and cheering are consistently heard, reflecting the warm and celebratory mood as the performance concludes.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Judging Panel—Simon Cowell in a black zip-up top, Amanda Holden in a pink shiny dress, Alesha Dixon in a silver sequined top, and David Walliams in a brown suit—seated at their desk, reacting with smiles and engagement. Simon Cowell humorously states, "This is proof that a dog can do dentistry," eliciting audience laughter, to which David Walliams adds, "Okay." The camera cuts to close-ups of Simon Cowell and David Walliams, both appearing amused and expressive. The scene shifts to the stage, where the Woman in Black Sequined Outfit and Top Hat, holding the White and Gray Speckled Dog, stands confidently under vibrant blue and purple lighting with dynamic geometric patterns in the backdrop. The dog, wearing a black vest with a gold star and red paw pads, appears calm and content as the woman smiles warmly. David Walliams announces, "We're gonna take a vote," followed by his enthusiastic declaration, "This is the easiest yes of all time!" as the audience cheers and a dramatic, emotional song begins to play. The judges react with delight, with Amanda Holden clapping and David Walliams laughing, as Amanda, Alesha, Simon, and David all vocally confirm, "It's a yes from me." The woman then walks across the stage, holding the dog, and the camera follows her movement. Simon Cowell expresses his enjoyment, "Oh, so enjoyed," and notes, "And we saw stuff we've never seen before." After the performance, she exits the stage, greeted by a man in a black outfit who pets the dog, while David Walliams asks her, "How did that feel out there?" The Woman in Black Sequined Outfit and Top Hat responds, "Oh, crazy dream come true, so wow," and Amanda Holden praises, "Oh, you did so well." The scene transitions to backstage, where the woman, still holding the dog, walks through a hallway with warm lighting and wooden paneling, accompanied by continued audience applause and cheering. The video concludes with a close-up of David Walliams, who is smiling and speaking into the microphone, encouraging, "Go encourage," as the on-screen graphic 'TOP TALENT DIANA VEDYASHINKA' appears, indicating the performer's name and talent category. The atmosphere throughout is lively and celebratory, with the audience visibly engaged and the judges showing genuine appreciation for the act, all set to the backdrop of the emotional song.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of the Britain's Got Talent stage, bathed in vibrant blue and purple lighting, as an upbeat, energetic background music plays. The judging panel, including Simon Cowell in a black zip-up top, Amanda Holden in a pink shiny dress, Alesha Dixon in a silver sequined top, and David Walliams in a brown suit, are seen reacting with smiles and amusement, while the audience claps and cheers enthusiastically. Simon Cowell stands and gestures animatedly, then is shown in a close-up speaking into the microphone with a serious expression, making a comment about having a TV show to make. The camera captures the audience's continued enthusiastic clapping and cheering. A woman, later identified as Diana, wearing a striking red satin outfit with a black sequined collar, walks confidently towards the center of the stage, with the Britain's Got Talent logo prominently displayed behind her. As she takes her position, David Walliams' voice is heard wishing her "Good luck, Diana," followed by a surge of applause and cheering. David then asks, "What's your name please?" to which Diana responds, "My name is Diana. I'm from St. Petersburg, Russia, and I'm gonna show you a dog act." Simon Cowell exclaims "Fantastic!" and David adds, "Oh my god, it's gonna love it." Simon continues by asking, "How long have you been training dogs for?" Diana replies, "For like 15 years." Simon then comments, "Okay, well we love dog acts. Is this something different to what we've seen before with the dog act?" Diana hopes so, saying, "I really hope so." Simon humorously adds, "I was gonna say do you have any pets, but obviously you do. So good luck," to which Diana gratefully responds, "Thank you very much," as the audience's applause swells once more.

The stage is vibrantly lit with blue and purple hues, featuring a large star backdrop and a cityscape projection of London landmarks. On-Screen Graphics introduce 'DIANA VEDYASHINKA, PERFORMER, 35' as she confidently walks onto the glossy stage in a red satin top and black skirt, accompanied by two small, black and tan dachshunds. As Diana guides her dogs through a choreographed routine involving small ramps and top hats, the energetic dachshunds run, jump, and weave with precision. The Audience erupts in cheers and applause, clapping along to the lively music, while the judges watch with growing amusement and delight, repeatedly commenting "She's so fun" and "Oh, I love it." The dynamic stage lighting enhances the spectacle with beams and reflections. The segment concludes with the dogs completing their well-rehearsed routine, leaving the judges visibly impressed, as David Walliams and Simon Cowell exchange amused glances, all against the prominent Britain's Got Talent Logo.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Simon Cowell clapping, setting a positive tone as the stage lights illuminate the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo and a vibrant cityscape backdrop, all set to an upbeat, lively background music. Diana Vedyashinka, dressed in a red satin top with a black sequined collar, a black skirt, and beige high heels, walks confidently onto the stage with two dachshunds. The stage is set with several top hats and small ramps, and the dogs immediately begin their routine, weaving through the obstacles with precision. Diana guides them with fluid movements, and the dogs perform synchronized jumps and runs, showcasing their training, as a judge exclaims, "That is amazing!" and later, "I love what they do, they're amazing!" The audience erupts in applause as the dogs leap onto the top hats, standing upright in a coordinated display. The judges, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, are visibly impressed, clapping and smiling, with one judge remarking, "Oh look!" and another encouragingly stating, "You could do it, you could do it, you could do it." The performance culminates with the dogs standing proudly on the hats, and Diana takes a bow as the audience cheers, the stage lighting shifting dynamically to enhance the spectacle, and the judges' reactions reflecting their delight and amazement at the well-choreographed act, all underscored by the continuous lively music and enthusiastic applause.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with close-ups of the judging panel, showing Simon Cowell engaged and amused, and Amanda Holden adjusting her hair and smiling, as the audience claps and cheers. A wide shot reveals the 'Britain's Got Talent' stage with its logo and the judges' desk. Diana Vedyashinka, in a red satin outfit, stands confidently on stage with two dachshunds in top hats, who perform a choreographed routine, running and weaving through obstacles under vibrant blue and purple lights. The judges react with amusement, David Walliams laughing heartily and Simon Cowell gesturing animatedly. As the performance concludes, the judges discuss the act. Simon Cowell is heard saying "Good, good, good, stay" and then prompts Amanda, "Uh, Amanda, you love that." Amanda enthusiastically responds, "Oh, I loved it so much! It was just the cutest thing, especially when they kind of rolled over, they were like the tide coming in." She continues, praising Diana, "You have done an amazing job with them. Like everybody knows, it's difficult just training one dog. You've got five of them. I honestly applaud you for that," to which Diana is heard responding "Thank you." David Walliams then takes the microphone, making a joke about getting a "ride home" and praising the "amazing dog acts" on the show, adding, "I sort of want to take them all home. They are absolutely beautiful." He then shares an "interesting fact" about dachshunds potentially replacing German Shepherd police dogs in the future, stating, "They are going to be replacing them with 50 of those to every one German Shepherd," which elicits a surprised "Really?" from another judge, likely Amanda. David concludes with a humorous warning, "You do not want to mess with 50 sausage dogs," as laughter and applause fill the air.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Simon Cowell, smiling and reacting, followed by a shot of a black dachshund on stage, hinting at a dog act. The scene then transitions to a wide view of the Britain's Got Talent stage, where judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams are seated, all four X buttons lit, indicating a negative response to a prior act. Simon exclaims "Wow!" before stating, "Right. Uh, we're gonna vote. David, yes or no?" David enthusiastically responds, "Of course, I'm gonna say yes!" amidst audience cheering. Amanda and Alesha both declare, "It's a yes from me!" Simon then congratulates the unseen act, saying, "You have four yeses. Congratulations." Upbeat, whimsical instrumental music begins as Diana Vedyashinka, a woman in a red satin top with a black sequined collar and black skirt, performs a choreographed routine with two dachshunds on stage, utilizing top hats and small ramps as props. She raises her arms triumphantly as the dogs complete their routine, accompanied by audience clapping and cheering. The scene cuts backstage to a man with a beard, wearing a leather jacket and holding a guitar, walking with two other men toward the stage, identified by on-screen graphics as 'Aaron and Buddy.' Simon says, "Yeah, okay," and David encourages, "Come on," with Simon echoing, "Come on." David adds, "Everybody would love to see him," and Simon replies, "Second, of course," followed by David's playful, "Can you be a dog?" which elicits audience laughter. Aaron steps onto the stage with his brown dog, Buddy, and begins to play an acoustic guitar, filling the stage with melodic music. The audience and judges react with smiles and applause. The performance continues with Aaron playing and Buddy standing attentively beside him, occasionally looking up. The stage backdrop features a vibrant cityscape with London landmarks, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple lighting. The judges are visibly engaged, with Simon leaning forward and smiling. Aaron greets the audience, "Hello. How are you?" and Simon replies, "I'm good, thank you. How are you?" before prompting, "Now, introduce the two of you, please." Aaron tells Buddy, "Okay, sit," and Buddy barks. Aaron then clarifies, "I know, not yet, not yet. We say, we say that for a minute. Right. Okay, I'm Aaron, and what's your name?" Buddy responds with another bark. The segment concludes with Aaron and Buddy on stage, the dog sitting calmly as Aaron strums his guitar under the bright stage lights and the glowing Britain's Got Talent logo.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Simon Cowell, smiling and reacting to a performance, followed by a shot of a black dachshund on stage, suggesting a dog act. The scene transitions to a wide view of the Britain's Got Talent stage, where the judges—Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams—are seated at their panel, with all four X buttons lit, indicating a negative response to a prior act. The audience is shown clapping and engaged. The focus shifts to Diana Vedyashinka, a woman in a red satin top with a black sequined collar and a black skirt, performing a choreographed routine with two dachshunds on stage, using top hats and small ramps as props. She raises her arms triumphantly as the dogs complete their routine. The scene then cuts to backstage, where a man with a beard, wearing a leather jacket and holding a guitar, walks with two other men toward the stage. On-screen graphics identify them as 'Aaron and Buddy.' The man, Aaron, steps onto the stage with his dog, Buddy, a brown dog with a black collar, and begins to play the guitar. The audience and judges react with smiles and applause. The performance continues with Aaron playing and Buddy standing attentively beside him, occasionally looking up at him. The stage backdrop features a vibrant cityscape with iconic London landmarks, illuminated by dynamic blue and purple lighting, enhancing the theatrical atmosphere. The judges, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, are visibly engaged, with Simon leaning forward and smiling. The segment concludes with Aaron and Buddy on stage, the dog sitting calmly as Aaron strums his guitar, under the bright stage lights and the glowing Britain's Got Talent logo.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens backstage, showing two men, one in a black shirt and the other in a striped sweater, reacting with surprise and amusement. The scene then cuts to the stage, illuminated by vibrant blue and purple lights, featuring a large star backdrop and a reflection of London’s skyline, including St. Paul’s Cathedral and Tower Bridge, along with the Britain's Got Talent logo. Aaron, a bearded man in a black leather jacket over a plaid shirt, stands holding an acoustic guitar with a Union Jack strap, alongside his brown dog, Buddy, who wears a black collar. Aaron praises Buddy, saying "good boy," as the dog sits attentively. From the judging panel, David Walliams, in a brown suit, exclaims with amusement, "Oh my god, he can speak!" Aaron clarifies, "That's Buddy, he's saying he's he's Buddy," to which Simon Cowell, in a black zip-up top, dryly responds, "I got that." Aaron playfully tells the judges, "Not yet, not yet. You're ruining the surprise," as Buddy performs a trick, lying down on command after Aaron says "Down. Down." David Walliams prompts Aaron, "Tell me why you're both here." Aaron explains that he and Buddy "have developed a real bond and he is now my best friend," adding humorously, "He's gorgeous. After my wife, might I add." He continues, setting up the act, "I was playing the guitar when he was about 12 weeks old and he just started what he's about to do. Sing." Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden in a pink shiny dress, and Alesha Dixon in a silver sequined top, all seated at the desk with red buzzers and microphones, react with a mix of skepticism and excitement. Simon asks, "Are you serious?" while Alesha exclaims, "You've been waiting for this," and Amanda adds, "This is his dream." Aaron confirms, "Pretty serious." David Walliams expresses incredulous amusement, "Sing? Sing? The dog is going to sing?" with Simon echoing, "Sing?" Alesha Dixon then reacts with an impressed "Amazing!" Aaron further explains that Buddy "likes to sing to songs," a talent they've "developed it over the months." The judges' excitement builds, with David Walliams declaring, "I am so excited," and Simon Cowell stating, "This could be history in the making here." Aaron responds, "Well, let's hope so." Amanda Holden adds, "I cannot wait for this." Aaron then gives Buddy commands, "Are you gonna come under? Come under. Under. Sit, sit," accompanied by acoustic guitar strumming, as Buddy moves around the stage, occasionally looking up at Aaron, demonstrating his training. The audience is visible in the background, clapping and enjoying the lively atmosphere, as the camera alternates between close-ups of the engaged judges, the performing duo, and the appreciative crowd. The performance concludes with Aaron and Buddy standing together on stage, the dog looking alert and well-trained, as the dynamic stage lighting continues to enhance the visual appeal, and the judges and audience respond positively.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Aaron, a bearded man in a black leather jacket over a plaid shirt, performing on stage with his brown dog Buddy. Aaron plays an acoustic guitar with a Union Jack strap, while Buddy stands attentively beside him, occasionally performing tricks like sitting and walking around. The stage features a glossy reflective floor and a large digital backdrop displaying London’s skyline under dynamic blue and purple lighting, all set to upbeat, lively performance music. The audience is visibly engaged, clapping and cheering enthusiastically. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where Amanda Holden, in a black dress with a sheer mesh top and a bow collar, speaks animatedly, gesturing with her hands. She expresses her love for the act, noting Aaron's animated performance and his effort to enunciate words, humorously suggesting that what she initially thought was a microphone might be a 'dog translator' to enhance the act, prompting laughter from the audience. Simon Cowell, in a dark suit and tie, listens intently, smiling, and engages with Amanda, asking if she's serious about the translator idea before agreeing that Aaron 'definitely needs one of them.' Alesha Dixon, wearing a brown and beige checkered jacket with a Union Jack scarf, laughs heartily at the exchange. Simon then offers his critique, stating that 'singing is a big word' for Aaron's performance, describing it as 'making some noises' while playing the guitar, which again elicits audience laughter. He admits he 'couldn't make out the individual lyrics' but concludes by calling Aaron and Buddy 'adorable' and praising their connection, thanking Aaron for bringing Buddy along as 'we've all fallen in love with him,' as the audience applauds. The segment alternates between close-ups of the judges’ reactions and wide shots of Aaron and Buddy on stage, maintaining a lively and entertaining atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Buddy, a brown dog wearing a Union Jack collar, standing attentively on stage beside Aaron, who holds an acoustic guitar with a matching Union Jack strap. The scene cuts to the judging panel, where Amanda Holden, in a black dress, declares, "Buddy's eyes and I just fell in love, so I'm saying yes," as Simon Cowell, in a gray sweater, animatedly gestures and adds, "You got me on hello. I'm gonna say yes." David Walliams, in a brown suit, smiles and nods, then humorously states, "Well look, based on the fact if this translator exists, uh, which means that when he barks he's actually singing, uh, I have to say yes." Alesha Dixon, in a checkered jacket with a Union Jack scarf, reacts positively as the audience claps and cheers under vibrant blue and purple stage lighting, accompanied by upbeat, celebratory music. The camera pans to a wide shot of the stage, revealing the illuminated 'Britain's Got Talent' logo against a London skyline backdrop, with Aaron playing guitar and Buddy standing beside him. The judges continue to engage, with Simon suggesting, "Also, he could give Dec a ride home tonight on his back," prompting laughter and cheers from the audience. Amanda thanks him, while David quips about his "translator" and getting them "for cats as well," to which Amanda adds, "My cat talks quite a lot." The segment concludes with a graphic overlay reading 'TOP TALENT' and 'TRIP HAZARD', highlighting a comedic moment amidst the lively atmosphere and engaged audience, as the music swells dramatically.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Lucy, a small, fluffy white dog wearing a red bow tie and white collar, held by Trip Hazard, a woman with blonde, wavy hair in a black sequined sleeveless top and black pants. Trip greets the audience with a clear "Hello, hello" as she walks confidently onto the Britain's Got Talent stage, which features a glossy, reflective floor and a vibrant digital backdrop of London’s skyline in blue and purple hues. Amanda Holden's voice asks the dog's name, to which Trip, speaking for Lucy, replies, "My name's Lucy." Amanda then asks the name of the "gorgeous dog," and Trip clarifies, "Um, his name is Trip Hazard," prompting a surprised "Trip Hazard!" from David Walliams. Trip confirms, "Well, Trip for short," and responds with a "Yeah" to Amanda's query about whether he's named because she trips over him, eliciting laughter and applause from the visibly engaged audience. As upbeat, dramatic music begins, Trip gently places Lucy on the floor, and the dog energetically trots across the stage, responding to her cues. Simon Cowell's voice interjects, asking for clarity, "And, and just to be clear, there's only one of them, yeah?" Trip confirms, "Yeah, there's only one dog," amidst more laughter and applause. Simon and Amanda offer "Good luck," to which Trip replies, "Thank you." The performance continues with Lucy running in circles to energetic, synth-driven music, showcasing her training, while the judging panel, including Simon Cowell who exclaims "Oh, too cute" and "Now I'm feeling optimistic," and Amanda Holden who adds "Good," reacts with amusement and interest. On-screen graphics display 'TRIP HAZARD & LUCY' and 'DOG TRAINER, 30'. Simon excitedly shouts "Oh, look!" as Lucy performs. The segment concludes with Trip and Lucy walking off stage to a backstage area with warm lighting and red curtains, greeted by crew members, all under the prominent Britain's Got Talent logo with dynamic lighting effects.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with Trip Hazard, a woman with blonde, wavy hair wearing a black sequined sleeveless top, black pants, and black boots, performing a dynamic dance routine on stage with her small, fluffy white dog, Lucy. Lucy, adorned with a red bow tie and white collar, trots and runs alongside Trip Hazard, responding to her cues with precision, all set to an upbeat, pop-style background music featuring a female vocalist singing lyrics like "He's the one stop shop, makes my candy pop." The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lighting, featuring a large digital backdrop displaying a stylized London skyline with landmarks like Big Ben and Tower Bridge, along with a glowing star motif. Trip Hazard executes various dance moves, including spins and bends, while Lucy follows closely, at one point leaping onto her back as she bends forward. The audience is audibly engaged, clapping and cheering along with the music, and their laughter is heard, particularly around the 0:39 mark. The judging panel—including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams—reacts with amusement and approval, with Simon Cowell giving a thumbs-up and Alesha Dixon smiling broadly, while David Walliams' distinctive laugh is clearly audible. Trip Hazard concludes the routine by holding Lucy in her arms, giving a thumbs-up to the audience, and sharing a joyful moment with the judges, as the lively music continues.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the brightly lit America's Got Talent stage, featuring dynamic blue and gold lighting and the show’s logo, as a woman in a patterned blouse and black pants, holding a microphone, enthusiastically introduces, "ready to meet Bogart? Okay, let's bring out Bogart!" The audience erupts in applause and cheers. A brown and white dog, Bogart, wearing a red bandana, then appears, prompting the woman to exclaim, "Ah!" Judge Howie Mandel asks, "Does Bogart compete in agility competitions?" to which the woman replies, "He does. He competes against four-legged dogs and he's out there with the best of them," as the audience applauds again. She encourages, "Well, we can't wait to see this. Good luck to you guys," before asking Bogart, "Are you ready to go, Bogart?" The dog responds with a clear "Woof!" and she joyfully exclaims, "Yes, my dog!" as applause swells. Upbeat, emotional background music with a female vocalist begins, singing "Hands, put your empty hands in mine. And scars, show me all the scars you hide." Bogart then performs an energetic agility routine, guided by the woman's hand signals and verbal cues, leaping over white hurdles, weaving through poles, and running through a blue tunnel with precision. The vocalist continues, "And hope, if you're broken, oh, I'm still open to you. Cause I'm gonna stand by you." The audience, visible in the background, watches intently and applauds as Bogart completes the course, ending in a sit beside the woman. The judging panel, including Simon Cowell, Heidi Klum, Howie Mandel, and Sofia Vergara, reacts with smiles and engagement, with Simon gesturing and Heidi clapping, while the music swells with the line, "Even if you break it down," underscoring the lively and celebratory atmosphere.

The stage is brightly lit with dynamic blue and gold lighting, featuring the show’s logo and a cityscape backdrop. A brown and white dog, wearing a red bandana, performs an energetic agility routine, leaping over white hurdles, weaving through poles, and running through a blue tunnel. The woman guides the dog with hand signals and verbal cues, showcasing their synchronized training. The audience, visible in the background, watches intently and applauds. The judging panel, including Simon Cowell, Heidi Klum, Howie Mandel, and Sofia Vergara, reacts with smiles and engagement, with Simon gesturing and Heidi clapping. The dog completes the course with precision, ending in a sit beside the woman, who then addresses the judges. The atmosphere is lively and celebratory, with the audience’s applause and the judges’ positive reactions underscoring the success of the performance.

The dog energetically jumps over hurdles, weaves through poles, and runs through the blue tunnel, demonstrating impressive training and coordination, all while the music continues with lyrics like "I'll be your eyes 'til your skin shines." The woman encourages the dog with hand signals and verbal cues, maintaining a close and responsive interaction. As the dog performs, a voice exclaims "Oh, look at this!" and later "Oh, oh!", likely from a judge or host, expressing admiration. After completing the course, the dog sits beside the woman, who kneels to pet it affectionately. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where Simon Cowell, Howie Mandel, and Heidi Klum are seen smiling and clapping, clearly impressed, with the audience's cheers and applause continuing loudly. Another voice encourages with "Come on!" as the music swells. The woman then holds up a blue sign displaying 'Bogart' above a pink paw print and 'SIMON' below, concluding the performance to enthusiastic applause and cheering from both the audience and judges, highlighting a successful and heartwarming act. The song continues with the vocalist singing "I'll be your arms, I'll be your side. And when you can't rise, I'll be your hands and feet, I'll be your side."

Upbeat pop music with female vocals and enthusiastic audience applause accompany the dog as it energetically navigates white hurdles and a blue tunnel, showcasing precision and training. Simon Cowell exclaims, "He's a little winner!" before the Host, a woman in a bright yellow top, excitedly calls out the dog's name, "Yes, Boggart, Boggart, Boggart!", a sentiment echoed by Simon. The Host then speaks at a podium, gesturing expressively, stating her feeling that "Boggart is going to be a big hit on America's Got Talent." She reveals a surprising detail, admitting she "totally forgot that he was just on three legs," noting that he moves "like any other dog" and finds it "quite incredible." The stage backdrop shifts between vibrant blue geometric patterns and a city skyline as the dog continues its routine, weaving through poles and responding to the handler’s cues, while the audience remains engaged, clapping and smiling. The Host then asks Simon for his thoughts, to which he replies, "What do you think?" before emphatically declaring, "I love Boggart!" He praises the handler for rescuing him, calling it "an amazing story" and expressing his happiness that it worked out well, concluding, "I love this act." The segment concludes with the judges’ panel in focus, with Simon Cowell making his point, and the Host reacting with a smile, indicating a positive reception of the performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens on a brightly lit 'America's Got Talent' stage, featuring a vibrant cityscape backdrop with dynamic blue and orange lighting. A woman with pink hair in a high ponytail, wearing a white blouse with blue and orange polka dots, black pants, and yellow sneakers, guides a black and white dog, later identified as Bogart, through an agility course. The dog, wearing a red bandana, energetically jumps over white hurdles and weaves through poles, while the woman encourages it with gestures and verbal cues. The audience, seated in tiered rows, claps and cheers, visibly excited. The judging panel watches intently, with Sofia Vergara, in a coral dress, expressing her admiration in a distinct accent, stating the performance was 'heartwarming, inspiring, and beautiful,' and that she's 'so happy because this is the place for people like you to come and show the world how you are.' The bald man in a denim jacket, identified as Howie, enthusiastically agrees, saying 'Absolutely.' The host, in a yellow top, then addresses him, 'Howie!' Howie continues, mentioning 'Bogart' by name and noting he 'showed up, did it, seems happy about doing it,' even jokingly asking, 'Am I boring him?' The host responds, 'He feels right at home here.' Howie then directly addresses the dog, 'Bogart, I'm talking to you. Bogart.' Sofia Vergara suggests, 'Maybe this will wake him up. Should we vote for him?' Howie quickly gives his 'yes' vote, 'Good idea. My vote is yes!' as the audience erupts in cheers and upbeat music swells. The host follows with 'I give you a second yes!' amidst continued audience excitement. Sofia Vergara adds, 'Absolutely! Your third yes, Bogart!' The host then prompts, 'And Simon?' leading to the man in the dark suit delivering the final 'Four yeses!' The dog completes the course with a final leap, and the audience erupts in applause, accompanied by celebratory music. The host concludes, 'Thank you so much!' as the segment ends with a wide shot of the lively stage.

The dog performs energetically, jumping over hurdles and weaving through poles, while the woman encourages it with gestures and verbal cues. The audience, seated in tiered rows, reacts with visible excitement, clapping and cheering. The woman in the yellow top, likely the host, speaks at a podium, gesturing expressively. The dog completes the course with a final leap, and the audience erupts in applause. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the stage, showing the full setup and the engaged audience, emphasizing the lively and entertaining atmosphere of the performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a woman in a patterned blouse and black pants guiding a black and white dog through an agility course on a stage with a vibrant cityscape backdrop, accompanied by upbeat, energetic music and the roar of an engaged audience. Backstage, the same woman, now with pink hair, is warmly greeted by a bald man in a dark suit, who, acting as a host, comforts her, saying, "I see you are emotional, come here, give me a hug, talk to me, how are you feeling right now?" The woman, visibly moved, responds, "We've been through so much with him, it's wonderful being able to share his story." The host then congratulates her, exclaiming, "Congratulations, you got yourself four yeses, all right!" The judging panel also shares their approval, with Sofia Vergara remarking, "It was beautiful," and Simon Cowell adding, "That's amazing." The scene then shifts to a brightly lit, whimsical candy-themed stage, where a young girl identified by on-screen graphics as 'ALEXA LAUENBURGER' performs with several dogs, including 'Nala' and 'Emma,' to a new, very upbeat pop-style music track featuring female vocals singing about a "candy man." The dogs execute coordinated movements, with their barks occasionally heard, as the audience claps enthusiastically, and the judges watch attentively, contributing to the lively and colorful theatrical spectacle.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an upbeat, circus-like instrumental track and enthusiastic crowd cheering as Alexa Lauenburger, a young girl in a sparkly pink outfit with a matching bow, directs multiple dogs through a choreographed performance on a vibrant, candy-themed stage adorned with oversized lollipops and marshmallows. The dogs, including black and white, brown and white, and small black breeds, perform synchronized movements like standing on hind legs and weaving under her precise hand gestures. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the grand Britain's Got Talent stage, with the judging panel and engaged audience visible. The focus then shifts to Ashleigh and her white dog Pudsey, with Ashleigh, wearing a leopard-print dress and contestant number '75054', being introduced by a male voice asking, "Hello, how are you?" Ashleigh, with a clear, friendly voice, replies, "I'm fine, thank you." She then introduces her dog, "I'm Ashleigh, and this is my dog Pudsey," to which the male voice repeats, "Pudsey." He praises, "He's gorgeous. How old is he?" Ashleigh responds, "He's six." The conversation continues with the male voice asking about their first meeting and Ashleigh's age, to which she replies, "I'm 16." The male voice, identified as Simon Cowell, then asks about their future aspirations if they were to win, stating, "I'm looking for Oscars. Do you think your dog and you could win an Oscar?" Ashleigh confidently replies, "Yes!" followed by a burst of audience applause and cheers. Simon notes, "Pudsey just pricked up at that, look!" and jokes, "He's doing an Oscar, he's like, 'Yeah!'" He concludes with, "Good. Best of luck," and Ashleigh thanks him, then gently encourages Pudsey with, "Pudsey, come on, Pudsey, good boy. Ready?" The stage backdrop features a dynamic blue LED screen with circular patterns, and the Britain's Got Talent logo is prominently displayed, emphasizing the scale and energy of the performance.

The scene transitions to the Britain's Got Talent stage, where Ashleigh, wearing a leopard-print dress with contestant number '75054', performs a dynamic dance routine with her white dog Pudsey. The stage is vibrant with a blue LED backdrop displaying the show's logo and sparkling effects. Ashleigh and Pudsey execute synchronized movements, including Pudsey standing on his hind legs, jumping, and running, all set to the upbeat, cartoonish 'Flintstones' theme song, featuring the iconic "Yabba Dabba Doo!" vocalization. The performance concludes with Ashleigh and Pudsey striking a final pose, as the lively audience continues to cheer and wave flags.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Simon Cowell, dressed in a white long-sleeve shirt, seated at the judging panel, reacting with amusement and surprise as the audience cheers loudly, with a male voice exclaiming "Brilliant!" The camera cuts to Alesha Dixon, wearing a black and white patterned dress with a light green trim, who smiles and gestures as she speaks, telling Ashleigh and Pudsey, "You and Pudsey are adorable. I've been dying to see a dancing dog. That was fantastic!" Ashleigh, in a leopard-print dress with contestant number '75054' pinned to her chest, holding her calm, well-groomed white dog Pudsey, briefly responds "Thank you!" in a high-pitched voice, before Alesha adds, "Well done. Good boy." The audience erupts in cheers and clapping. Simon Cowell, now holding a pen and gesturing, speaks with a distinct voice, declaring, "Everything about that was brilliant. The chemistry between the two of you. I was completely blown away," to which Ashleigh again briefly replies, "Thank you!" amidst more audience cheering. Simon continues, "Uh, you know me, I love a dancing dog. I do. And Pudsey's one of the best dancing dogs I've ever seen," prompting further audience applause. He adds, "You've got this great connection between the two of you," before humorously suggesting, "My only criticism would have been, I would have put Pudsey in a prehistoric outfit as well," which elicits laughter from the audience. Simon then asks, "Do you think Pudsey would change if, if he won the show?" Alesha Dixon chimes in, "I think he could go a bit diva maybe. Hello, bit, he gets what he wants," as the audience laughs again. The camera returns to Ashleigh and Pudsey, who are both smiling and looking toward the judges, while Simon is seen gently holding Pudsey, showing affection. Simon concludes, "Okay, well we're gonna have to vote now. Uh, David, yes or no?" and an off-screen voice responds, "A million percent yes. Love it." The segment concludes with Ashleigh speaking and gesturing, while Pudsey remains calm and attentive on the stage, with its dynamic blue LED backdrop featuring circular patterns, as the on-screen text 'More great clips at itv.com/talent' is consistently displayed.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a dimly lit backstage area, bathed in vibrant purple and blue neon lighting, featuring metal truss structures, stage equipment, a large star-shaped backdrop, and a wooden case labeled 'MORE'. Three individuals—a man in a black sweater, a man in a checkered shirt and glasses, and a woman in a floral blouse—are interacting with a large, fluffy brown dog. The man in the black sweater greets, asking, "Hello there. You met our boy?", to which the man in the checkered shirt introduces the dog as "My boy, Horse." The man in the black sweater expresses surprise at its size, "I've never seen a dog so big," and the woman in the floral blouse reveals, "Well he weighs in at 81 kilos," prompting a "What!" from the man in black. He then explains they "came across these a few years ago." As upbeat, quirky background music plays, the group decides to get down with the dog, with the man in black sweater asking, "Should we all get down?" and the man in checkered shirt agreeing, "We can get down, I think. We can manage." The man in black sweater then sets the playful rule: "if you just switched on, anything the dog does, we've got to do." The dog, Horse, initially standing, is gently guided to lie down as the group kneels around it. Horse barks and playfully growls, engaging with the group. The man in the black sweater playfully lies on his back, and Horse jumps onto his chest, prompting laughter and affectionate reactions. The man in black sweater observes, "Oh, oh, oh, he's nibbling his paw," and later, "Guys, Hagrid's closed his eyes," referring to the dog. Horse then playfully rolls over and interacts with each person in turn, who respond with smiles and gentle petting. The man in black sweater notes, "Oh, he's come up," and "Oh, oh, oh, we're licking each other," while the man in checkered shirt jokingly declines, "I'm not doing that to you." The woman in floral blouse reacts with a surprised "What?" The man in black sweater then playfully remarks, "Oh, that's my wife. That's his wife," as Horse continues to bark and interact. The atmosphere is warm and lighthearted, with the group clearly enjoying Horse’s playful energy, all set against the backdrop of a behind-the-scenes moment, possibly before or after a performance, with the 'MORE' branding indicating a possible connection to a show or event. Horse's thick, shaggy coat and high energy are evident, as is its training, responding to cues and engaging with the people around it. The group’s casual yet neat clothing, including the woman’s floral blouse and the men’s button-downs, adds personality to the scene, with the consistent soft glow of the lighting highlighting the dog’s movements and the group's interactions.

The camera then cuts to a wide shot of the Britain's Got Talent stage, where the dog walks confidently across the glossy floor toward the judging panel. The judges — Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams — are seated at their illuminated desk, reacting with visible amusement and excitement. The audience is shown clapping and cheering, clearly entertained. The scene transitions back to the judges, who are now standing and applauding, with Simon Cowell gesturing animatedly and David Walliams raising his arms in approval. The atmosphere is lively and joyful, capturing a moment of shared delight between the performers, the dog, and the audience.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a brightly lit stage with a dynamic blue LED backdrop featuring a city skyline, including St. Paul’s Cathedral and Tower Bridge, and a glossy reflective floor. A man in a checkered shirt and brown pants stands hand-in-hand with a woman in a floral blouse and dark pants. The man explains they are here to attempt a brand new Guinness World Record, prompting cheers and applause from the audience. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where Simon Cowell reacts with visible excitement, raising his hand and smiling, while David Walliams expresses his eagerness to hear about the record. The man reveals the record involves 'catching sausages,' to which David enthusiastically declares, 'I'm in!' The man then introduces the woman, who in turn introduces their large, shaggy brown dog, 'Hagrid,' as the audience erupts in loud cheers and music swells, accompanied by the dog's barks. David asks for further explanation, and the man clarifies they need an adjudicator for this new record. David identifies one of the men in dark suits seen backstage as 'Chris,' the adjudicator, as Simon comments on his 'very smart' appearance and David adds, 'Good beard. Nice blazer.' The adjudicator, now on stage, officially states he is there to officiate the fastest time to catch ten items by a dog. As the judges, including Amanda Holden and Alesha Dixon, react with amusement and applause, the dog leaps playfully onto the man in the checkered shirt, who laughs and pets it. The dog then rolls over, interacting with the woman, who kneels to pet it, and a man in a black sweater who lies on his back, laughing as the dog jumps on him. Simon Cowell wipes his eye in delight, and David Walliams raises his arms in excitement, wishing the team 'Good luck.' The adjudicator begins a countdown, 'Three...', as the video ends with a wide shot of the joyful scene on stage, with the judges still engaged in the foreground.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a wide shot of a brightly lit stage, featuring a large blue star backdrop and a digital display of London’s skyline, as an unseen announcer's commanding voice counts down, "Two, one, go!" A digital timer above the stage begins counting up from 00:00. On stage, a Man in Checkered Shirt interacts with a Large Fluffy Brown Dog, while a bearded Man in Dark Suit officiates and a Woman in Floral Blouse stands by. The audience in the foreground immediately responds with excited laughter and applause. Close-ups show judges Simon Cowell and David Walliams smiling and laughing, their amusement echoed backstage by two laughing men in dark shirts. The announcer's voice continues counting, "One, Two, Three," as the dog performs various tricks like jumping and rolling, prompting more audience engagement. A male voice asks, "Right, you ready?" followed by a "Yeah" and instructions to "start now" in ten seconds. The counting resumes, with some confusion, "One, Two, Three, Stop, Four," leading to David Walliams asking, "Is that a record?" and Simon Cowell responding, "I don't know either, I was just counting." After confirming readiness, the announcer restarts the countdown, "Three, two, one, go!" and continues counting the dog's actions up to "Nine." As the timer reaches 13:08, the Man in Dark Suit raises his arms in approval, and the audience claps and cheers enthusiastically. The segment concludes with the dog lying down, being petted by both the Man in Checkered Shirt and the Woman in Floral Blouse under vibrant blue and purple stage lighting, accompanied by dramatic, building orchestral music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an upbeat, dramatic orchestral score accompanying a dynamic performance on the Britain's Got Talent stage. A large, shaggy brown dog energetically leaps and circles around a man in a checkered shirt and brown pants, who is laughing and engaging playfully with the highly trained animal. The judging panel, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, reacts with visible excitement, clapping, laughing, and raising their arms in approval, while the audience cheers and claps loudly. The stage features a glossy reflective floor and a large star backdrop with a digital display of London’s skyline. As the performance concludes with the dog playfully rolling over, the man in the checkered shirt, a woman in a floral blouse, and another woman in a striped top gather on stage. An announcer's voice confirms a "finish time of 14.7 seconds" and declares it "a brand new Guinness World Records title," prompting David Walliams to exclaim, "Absolutely amazing!" and Amanda Holden to shout, "How did you do it?!" Simon Cowell then offers congratulations, and the man in the checkered shirt responds, "Thank you very much." Simon continues, praising their dedication to "something so worthwhile," leading to audience laughter. He then initiates the voting, asking "Yes or no, David?", to which David, Amanda, and Alesha all give their "Yes" votes, with Alesha adding, "It's a yes from me." Simon humorously adds, "No more sausages," confirming "It's four yeses" before saying "Well done" amidst continued cheering and applause. The scene transitions backstage, where the man in the checkered shirt and the woman in the floral blouse walk through a dimly lit area with purple and blue neon lighting and metal truss structures. David Walliams asks if they're "over the moon," and the man in the checkered shirt replies, "We absolutely are," as the announcer begins to introduce "Kristy and Judy," maintaining the lively and celebratory atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The scene opens in a dimly lit backstage area, illuminated by purple and blue neon lighting, featuring metal truss structures and a large star-shaped backdrop. A group of four people and a large, shaggy brown dog are present, engaged in a lighthearted and playful challenge. The Man in Black Sweater, wearing a black sweater, initiates the activity, speaking clearly as he explains, "We're gonna try something out... We are gonna see how quick you can throw things in my mouth." He discusses the rules, mentioning a minimum distance, then dismisses it, adding, "Now obviously, a record has been set for throwing grapes in someone's mouth, has it?" The Man in Checkered Shirt, with gray hair and glasses, holding a small container of green treats, responds, "Not yet, but that needs to be researched and guidelines need to be written first," to which the Man in Black Sweater confirms, "Right, for the official attempt." Amidst laughter and excited chatter from the group, the Man in Black Sweater asks, "David, are you ready?" and the Man in Checkered Shirt replies, "I'm ready." With a clear countdown of "Three, two, one, go!", a rapid-fire game begins, marked by distinct "ding" sounds and enthusiastic counting from the group, reaching "Nine." The Large Fluffy Brown Dog, initially calm, becomes the playful focal point, jumping onto the Man in Black Sweater, who laughs heartily, and then rolling over on the floor. The Woman in Floral Blouse, with long brown hair, kneels to pet the dog, laughing and contributing to the excited atmosphere, while the Man in Checkered Shirt offers treats. The Man in Dark Suit (Backstage), in a dark suit with a Guinness World Records badge, observes the joyful interaction, occasionally adjusting his suit and smiling. The game culminates in a flurry of rapid "ding" sounds, a distinct buzzer, and a triumphant "Yeah!" from the group, followed by celebratory cheers and laughter, with the Man in Black Sweater exclaiming, "I thought you'd never do it!" The dog's playful antics and the group's joyful reactions create an engaging and fun moment.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens backstage at a talent show, where a group including a man in a black sweater, a woman in a floral blouse, and a man in a checkered shirt interact playfully with a large, shaggy brown dog. Male voices are heard discussing a time of "53.2 seconds" and whether they "can believe what we just done," accompanied by laughter. A male host's voice then exclaims, "With all that excitement, Hagrid must be getting really hungry!" The scene transitions to a brightly lit stage with a large star backdrop and 'Britain's Got Talent' branding, as dramatic, upbeat music swells and the audience erupts in applause and cheering. A white dog, wearing a Union Jack bow tie, is led confidently across the stage by a woman in a red top. She introduces her dog, stating, "My dog's called Miracle, and today we're hoping to show you a real miracle," before adding, "Okay, we're done. Thank you." The dog barks playfully as the woman interacts with it, saying, "Hello. You good? Okay. There we are." The host, the man in the black sweater, greets the dog warmly. The dog then approaches the judging panel, where Simon Cowell, in a white shirt and glasses, reacts with amusement, laughing and wiping his eye. The woman in red addresses Simon, asking, "Now Simon, I know that you love dogs?" to which Simon replies, "Yeah." She continues, revealing, "So, in front of you, are 40 of my dogs that are back home," prompting Simon to exclaim, "You've got 40?" She corrects him, "I have over 40." She then instructs him, "I would like you to cut those photos into four roughly equal piles," to which Simon responds, "Okay." Dynamic stage lighting illuminates a backdrop featuring London’s skyline, including Tower Bridge and St. Paul’s Cathedral, as suspenseful music plays while Simon begins the task. On-screen graphics display 'TOP TALENT' and 'AMANDA & MIRACLE'.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a white dog wearing a Union Jack bow tie, standing confidently on a glossy stage with a large star backdrop, as soft, mystical background music plays. A female voice, belonging to the Woman in Red Outfit, begins instructing, "Now I want you to take one from each pile from the top. Don't show me." The scene quickly shifts to the judging panel, where the woman in a red outfit is seen arranging stacks of white cards on a black mat, while a man in a white shirt observes. The female voice continues, "Keep one for yourself and pass three to Amanda. Just don't show me. And Amanda, if you can do the same. You keep one, pass that to David as well." Close-ups capture hands sorting the cards, emphasizing the preparation for a decision or scoring process. The judges, including a man with glasses and a woman in a red top, are shown reacting with focused expressions as the female voice explains, "Now there's no possible way I could know which card you've chosen. Okay? So you can see that they are all completely individual. Okay?" The scene transitions to a wide shot of the stage, revealing the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo prominently displayed on a large screen with dynamic lighting and a cityscape backdrop. The female voice then poses a question, "Now you may wonder why I have those forty dogs," before stating, "This is going to explain the story," as the background music becomes more emotional and melancholic. A man in a black shirt is seen kneeling beside the white dog, which is now wearing a different bow tie. The video then cuts to a black screen with a photo of the white dog, identified as Miracle, in a grassy field, accompanied by text stating, 'Miracle has been at the heart of our family for 6 years,' followed by 'But he wasn't always as happy and healthy as he is today.' During this emotional transition, a male voice exclaims, "I do! I do!" with another male voice echoing, "I do!", likely from the judging panel, reacting to the unfolding narrative. The overall atmosphere is one of anticipation and emotional depth, blending the excitement of a talent show with a heartfelt narrative about the dog's journey, underscored by the evolving music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with close-ups of an emotional audience, including a blonde woman with glasses and a man in a floral shirt, visibly moved as a male vocalist begins a slow, emotional acoustic song with gentle guitar accompaniment. On-screen text reveals a harrowing backstory: 'He spent the early part of his life in unimaginable conditions... as a victim of the dog meat trade.' Judge Simon Cowell, in a white shirt and glasses, covers his mouth in disbelief, audibly exclaiming "Oh my God." More text appears: 'He was discovered crushed in a cage with over 1000 other dogs,' accompanied by a disturbing image of a white dog hanging limply in a rusted cage, surrounded by others, intensifying the somber mood set by the music. The narrative shifts to hope with text 'Believe it or not, this is him,' followed by a shot of the same dog, now healthy and wearing a red bandana, lying calmly on stage beside a man in a black sweater who gently pets him, as the song's lyrics about "true colors" resonate. The audience and judges, including Amanda Holden in a red top and Alesha Dixon in a patterned outfit, are shown again, visibly emotional. Text details the rescue: 'When the dog rescuer first saw him she thought he was dead... But when her camera flashed... he opened an eye. It was a miracle he was still alive,' followed by a shot of the dog looking frail but alive in a recovery room with green and yellow walls. The segment concludes with Alesha Dixon, now in a gold and black headwrap, with tears streaming down her face, reflecting the profound emotional impact of the story, as the poignant song ends with the word "Beautiful."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with close-ups of emotional audience members, including a woman with dark hair and hoop earrings covering her mouth in awe, and another woman with blonde hair and glasses, visibly moved, as a male vocalist begins a heartfelt, piano-driven rendition of 'True Colors.' The song's poignant lyrics, 'If this world makes you crazy, take all you can bear. You call me up 'cause you know I'll be there. And I see your true colors shining through... True colors are beautiful,' accompany the presentation of a white dog named Miracle on stage by a man in a black shirt. On-screen text overlays narrate Miracle's rescue story: 'We had to save him,' 'We brought him back to Scotland and slowly nurtured him back to health,' and 'These days it's us who need him.' Images of Miracle in joyful states—running through a grassy yard, wearing a Union Jack bow tie, and smiling with his tongue out—are shown, contrasting with earlier visuals of him as a frail, rescued white dog. The narrative expands to reveal that out of 40 dogs, 19 are rescue dogs, illustrated with images of other dogs. The judges, including Amanda Holden in a red top and Alesha Dixon in a black and white patterned dress, react emotionally to the song and story, with Amanda crying and Alesha wiping tears. The stage, illuminated with bright lights and the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo, provides the backdrop for this moving moment. As the song fades, the audience erupts in loud, sustained clapping and cheering, while the judges' panel, including Simon Cowell and David Walliams, are visibly moved, their reactions displayed above their 'X' buttons.

segments.integrated_caption: Amidst applause and cheering, the segment opens with a close-up of the Woman in Red Outfit, her long dark hair framing her face, speaking emotionally in a soft voice. She explains, "I have no idea which photos you chose, but Miracle did," before introducing a pre-filmed segment. The scene transitions to the full Britain's Got Talent stage, illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lights, featuring the show’s logo and a London skyline backdrop. The Woman in Red Outfit, in a red satin top and black pants, stands beside Miracle, a white dog wearing a red bandana. In the pre-filmed clip, she kneels to ask Miracle, "Right, Miracle? We've only got room to take four dogs to go and meet the Britain's Got Talent judges. So who's it gonna be? Who do you want to take? Tell me." Miracle responds with barks and whines, prompting her praise, "Good boy!" Gentle, emotional background music begins as the Woman in Red Outfit thanks "guys." Back on stage, she addresses David Walliams, "Can I ask you to hold up the photo that you have and call out the name of the dog?" The Judging Panel, including a surprised Simon Cowell and David Walliams, is shown. The judges then hold up cards featuring Miracle's photo alongside celebrity names. David Walliams, with a clear voice, announces "Braveheart" and later "Jean Harlow." Alesha Dixon declares "Marilyn Monroe," and Amanda Holden, appearing tearful, says "Grace Kelly." Simon Cowell covers his mouth in shock, and Amanda Holden appears visibly emotional, underscoring the performance's impact. The segment concludes with the judges holding their cards, the stage dramatically lit, and the audience watching intently, as Miracle, now sporting a Union Jack bow tie, interacts with the Woman in Red Outfit and a Man in Black Shirt.

The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lights, featuring the show’s logo and a backdrop of London’s skyline. The Woman in Red Outfit walks Miracle across the stage, and the judges are shown holding up cards with celebrity names—Simon holds 'BRAVEHEART', Alesha Dixon holds 'MARILYN MONROE', Amanda Holden holds 'GRACE KELLY', and Alesha Dixon also holds 'JEAN HARLOW'—each card featuring a photo of Miracle. The video cuts to a home setting where the Woman in Red Outfit kneels to affectionately pet Miracle, who wags his tail joyfully. Back on stage, the Woman in Red Outfit and a Man in Black Shirt interact with Miracle, who is now wearing a Union Jack bow tie. The judges react with visible emotion, with Simon covering his mouth in shock and Amanda Holden appearing tearful. The segment concludes with the judges holding up their cards, emphasizing the emotional connection between Miracle and the celebrities, while the stage remains bathed in dramatic lighting and the audience watches intently.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the Woman in Red Outfit, her long dark hair framing her face as she speaks emotionally on stage, wearing a red satin top and black pants. Over soft, emotional background music, she explains, "Obviously, I couldn't bring 40 that are there, but Miracle decided to choose four. So I'm going to let you meet them." The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple lights, featuring a large star backdrop and a digital London skyline. The scene then cuts to the Judging Panel, where Simon Cowell smiles broadly, while Amanda Holden and Alesha Dixon react with visible delight. As upbeat pop music with a female vocalist singing "Every little thing she does is magic" begins, the Woman in Red Outfit leads Miracle, a white dog with a black nose, out from a whimsical doghouse labeled 'MIRACLE'. Miracle, initially wearing a red bandana, runs energetically across the glossy stage, followed by two smaller dogs—one white with a black bow tie and another brown and white with a red collar. The dogs perform a choreographed routine, weaving around the stage as the Woman in Red Outfit guides them with hand signals, while the audience erupts in cheers and applause. Close-ups show the audience's joyful reactions as the performance continues. The Woman in Red Outfit then speaks again, playfully naming the dogs or celebrity inspirations over the music and applause, saying, "So we have... Jean Harlow... Grace Kelly... Three parts..." eliciting laughter. Alesha Dixon is seen holding up a card with 'JEAN HARLOW' written on it, aligning with the spoken names. The camera pans to backstage, where two men in dark suits laugh and gesture excitedly, as the dogs continue their energetic routine, running across the reflective floor. The Woman in Red Outfit interacts affectionately with Miracle, who wags his tail vigorously, all to the backdrop of intensifying music, cheers, and applause. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the stage, showing the dogs running toward the judges, who are visibly moved. Alesha Dixon holds up a card, while Simon Cowell covers his mouth in amazement, asking "What is he gonna do?" The music reaches a crescendo before fading slightly, and the Woman in Red Outfit says "Hello," as the atmosphere remains electric, filled with laughter, applause, and emotional responses from both the audience and judges.
