segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Alesha Dixon, seated at the judging panel in a black leather jacket and gold chain, her expression attentive and slightly amused as she greets the stage, asking, "How are you?" The camera cuts to Marc Métral, identified by on-screen text as 'MARC MÉTRAL, ENTERTAINER, 61', who is visually a man in a brown suit with a bow tie, standing on stage in front of a vibrant digital backdrop of London’s skyline. He responds with a French accent, "Good, thank you," and introduces himself, "My name is Marc Métral, I come from Paris." Alesha playfully replies, "Bonjour!" and asks what he'll do. Marc announces, "I have a special guest for you," and invites "Miss Wendy, come on!" as upbeat, dramatic music swells and the audience erupts in cheers and applause. A small, fluffy white dog, Wendy, bursts onto the stage, running energetically across the glossy floor. Marc sings "Oh Wendy" as the dog is briefly held by a woman in a blue top before being placed on a silver pedestal. Simon Cowell's expression shifts from neutral to surprised. Marc, now standing beside the calm dog, adjusts his tie and smiles, telling Wendy, "Sit down," and reassuring her, "you can't fall down, don't worry." Alesha comments, "The dog was cute," prompting laughter from the audience. Marc asks the audience, "Isn't she lovely?" to more cheers and applause. He then addresses Wendy, asking her to "say good evening" and "speak English." After a moment, Marc prompts, "Come on, hey, say something." The dog then makes a muffled, low growl/bark-like sound that distinctly sounds like "No," causing the audience to gasp, then explode in laughter and cheers, while Marc reacts with an exaggerated sound of surprise. The stage lighting remains dynamic with blue and purple hues, and the judges' desk features illuminated 'X' symbols throughout the entertaining act.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with Marc Métral, a man in a brown suit with a bow tie, standing on a brightly lit stage beside a small, fluffy white dog perched on a silver pedestal. The backdrop features a dynamic digital display of London’s skyline with vibrant blue and purple lighting. Marc, speaking with a male voice and a possible French accent, gestures animatedly, asking the dog, "but why not it's fantastic, come on," then prompts, "presentez-vous, Deutsch?" The dog, with a high-pitched, squeaky ventriloquist voice, replies "Yes," immediately drawing laughter and applause from the audience. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where Simon Cowell is seen laughing heartily, covering his mouth, while Amanda Holden appears emotional, exclaiming "Oh my god." Marc continues, asking the dog "English?" to which the dog humorously responds "See" (sounding like "Oui"). More audience laughter and applause follow as Marc asks "Spanish?" and the dog again replies "Oui." When Marc asks "Français?", the dog unexpectedly says "Okay." The audience reacts with amusement and excitement, including a woman in the front row covering her mouth in surprise, while Simon Cowell's hearty laugh is distinctly heard. Marc returns, holding a small object, and addresses the dog directly, "Hey, Wendy, listen to me. You think I am stupid?" Wendy replies "Yes," prompting another wave of laughter and applause. David Walliams, with an excited voice, declares, "This is brilliant! I want one!" Marc continues his interaction, asking Wendy if she wants to speak, to which she initially says "No," then "Oh yes, I want." He then asks if she knows how to sing, and after a brief "No" and Marc's "What?", Wendy enthusiastically replies "Yes, yes, yes." The stage lighting shifts between cool blues and warm purples, enhancing the theatrical atmosphere. Brief cuts to backstage areas show two men in dark suits conversing and laughing, reflecting the lighthearted mood. As Marc encourages, "Well, we listen to you," Wendy delivers a surprising "Meow," leading to more audience laughter and applause. The performance concludes with Marc bowing slightly, the dog remaining poised on the pedestal, and the judges’ reactions indicating a positive reception, with Marc remarking, "It's about wow, eh?" The Britain's Got Talent logo is visible on the backdrop.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Alesha Dixon, seated at the judging panel, smiling warmly as she interacts with a small fluffy white dog on a pedestal. The camera then cuts to a wide shot of the Britain's Got Talent stage, showcasing the full set with the judges' panel and the audience in a dimly lit theater, all against a vibrant digital display of London’s skyline illuminated in blue and purple hues. David Walliams, dressed in a brown suit with a bow tie, stands beside the small white dog on its silver pedestal, playfully asking it, "you understand?" The dog responds with a high-pitched "Meow" as the audience erupts in laughter and applause. David then addresses the audience, saying, "Music, please. We're gonna sing something for you, all right?" He instructs the dog, "Okay, don't look at me, look at the audience, legs up," before comically singing the word "Feelings" in a slightly off-key, operatic style, encouraging the audience to join in. He continues to sing parts of the song, including "Nothing more, nothing more, nothing more, trying to forget," with a humorous, exaggerated delivery. He prompts the audience again, "All together, okay? Please, come on. It's Britain's Got Talent, okay? All together." The judges, including Simon Cowell and Amanda Holden, are visibly entertained, smiling and laughing. David then launches into a more sustained, dramatic rendition of "Feelings," drawing out the notes with a theatrical "Aiy-aiy-aiy!" as the audience cheers and claps. He continues to sing, adding a comedic "Oh my God!" and further vocalizations, while interacting with the dog, lifting it gently and holding it close. The atmosphere is lighthearted and joyful, with the stage lighting enhancing the vibrant backdrop. The segment concludes with David Walliams smiling broadly, holding the calm dog, as the audience continues to cheer and applaud, and the judges remain engaged and amused by his performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the vibrant Britain's Got Talent stage, featuring a glossy reflective floor and a dynamic digital backdrop of London’s skyline, including Tower Bridge and St. Paul’s Cathedral, illuminated with blue and purple lighting. Triumphant orchestral music with a choir swells as David Walliams, dressed in a brown suit with a bow tie, playfully interacts with a small, fluffy white dog on a silver pedestal. David gestures animatedly, prompting the dog to run across the stage, eliciting laughter and amusement from the audience, whose cheers and applause are clearly audible. David exclaims "Amazing!" before asking, "What's her name?" A female voice, identified as Amanda Holden, responds "Wendy," and David repeats the name. The camera cuts to Simon Cowell, seated at the judging panel, who bursts into hearty laughter, his expression shifting from neutral to delighted. Amanda Holden, in a red top, and Alesha Dixon, in a black leather jacket with a gold chain, are also seen smiling and reacting warmly. Amanda, with an emotional tone, explains that Simon has "waited a long time for this moment," revealing that "for nine years, Simon has said he wanted a dog that could meow or sing. And your dog did both those things," as the audience erupts in loud applause and cheers. Amanda continues, stating, "you're making television history here." Simon then expresses his amazement, saying "Wow," and adding, "I don't know how she does that, how you taught her to do it, but it was incredible." David Walliams responds with "Oh, thank you so much," before Simon further questions, "How does she know the words?" David proudly concludes, "She's very clever." The judges’ desk is modern, with illuminated 'X' symbols and red buzzers, and the audience, visible in the dimly lit theater, continues to clap and laugh, with one woman in the front row covering her mouth in delight. The camera pans across the stage, showing the full set with the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo prominently displayed, before focusing on David Walliams again, who continues to engage with the dog and the audience, his gestures expressive and theatrical, maintaining the lively and humorous atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with close-ups of the judging panel, where Simon Cowell, in a dark suit and tie, Alesha Dixon, in a black leather jacket with a gold chain necklace, and David Walliams, in a brown suit with a bow tie, react with smiles and expressions of amusement. Simon's distinct male voice declares, "This is an incredible act and one we're never going to forget, Mark. Well done," followed by David's "You're very welcome." Alesha adds, "Fantastic act, Mark. Really something special," as applause and cheering fill the air. Simon then prompts, "Right, David," who confidently states, "Well, this is a very easy yes." This elicits loud cheers and applause from the audience, including a woman in a floral blouse covering her mouth in delight and another in a black top clapping enthusiastically. Alesha's voice then rings out, "It's a yes from me!" twice, met with even more intense cheering. Simon concludes the judging by announcing, "You've got four big fat yeses," as upbeat, celebratory pop music with female vocals begins, accompanying the continued applause. The camera cuts to a wide shot of the grand theater, illuminated with blue and purple lights, showcasing the large, cheering audience and ornate architecture. David Walliams is then seen backstage, laughing and exclaiming, "Oh, that's amazing!" as he's playfully patted on the head by two men in dark shirts. Simon's voice congratulates, "Congratulations!" and Alesha adds, "That was fantastic!" David chimes in with "Brilliant, well done!" and Simon calls it "Unbelievable." David then humorously questions, "How do you get a dog to do that?" as he walks through a hallway marked 'BACKSTAGE' with a 'BRITAIN'S GOT TALENT' sign, while various indistinct voices offer thanks. The judges are shown again, with Simon now seated and smiling, while Alesha and David continue to react positively. The video concludes with Simon Cowell relaxing on a beige couch in a lounge area, holding a silver iPhone and smiling as he looks at the screen, with a red cushion and a black bag beside him. He's heard discussing the act, stating, "We found a dog who sang and talked," with David interjecting, "Well, we found a dog that can say..." before Simon cuts in, "No, we haven't." Alesha's voice is heard saying, "I love that Simon's telling everyone," and Simon then asks someone named Lauren, "Lauren, are you sitting down?" to which a female voice replies, "Yeah," before Simon continues, "A dog came in..."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Simon Cowell relaxing on a beige couch, holding a silver iPhone, alongside David Walliams who smiles while holding a drink. Initial audio features a cat meowing sound effect, followed by someone exclaiming, "Oh my god, are you serious? I swear, and meowed," and laughter, with a voice clarifying, "The dog can't actually meow." David Walliams then transitions through a grand, ornate hallway with a golden staircase and a 'Britain's Got Talent' sign. The scene shifts to the brightly lit stage of 'Italia's Got Talent,' where Chico, a man in a yellow t-shirt and jeans, stands with his brown and white dog, Joe. A host or judge asks in Italian, "Sarei, che cosa farete stasera a Italia's Got Talent?" (What will you do tonight at Italia's Got Talent?). Chico responds in Italian, "Eh, vorremmo giocare come facciamo tutti i giorni e vorremmo invitare qualcuno che parla inglese" (We would like to play as we do every day, and we would like to invite someone who speaks English), as dramatic music builds. From the judging panel, Joel, a bald man in a brown jacket, volunteers, saying "Io" (Me) and "Sì, parlo" (Yes, I speak). He asks, "Devo venire da voi?" (Should I come to you?), to which Chico replies, "Magari, se vuoi giocare con noi ci fa piacere" (Maybe, if you want to play with us, we'd be happy), prompting audience applause and laughter. Joel approaches the stage, and a panelist exclaims, "Guarda c'è un amichetta Chico, eh!" (Look, there's a little friend, Chico, eh!). Joel greets Chico, "Come stai?" (How are you?), and then addresses the dog, "Chico, guarda chi c'è, Joe!" (Chico, look who's here, Joe!). Joel then speaks to Joe in English, "What's up, mate?" and "I speak English," while Joe barks enthusiastically. Joel makes dog sounds and playfully asks, "Are you Australian?" as the audience claps and laughs. Chico confirms with "Sì, sì, sì." Joel pets Joe warmly, and Chico gives commands, "Prova un po' a camminare, Joe, vieni" (Try to walk a little, Joe, come). The stage backdrop displays the 'Italia's Got Talent' logo in vibrant blue and purple lighting, with on-screen graphics like 'What are you going to do tonight?' and 'We'd like to involve someone that speaks English' appearing, enhancing the lively and playful atmosphere.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with Simon Cowell and David Walliams relaxing on a beige couch in a lounge area, with Simon holding a silver iPhone and David smiling while holding a drink. The setting then shifts to the stage of 'Italia's Got Talent,' where a man in a yellow t-shirt and jeans, identified as Chico, stands with his brown and white dog. Chico speaks into a microphone, asking for someone who speaks English to join the act. The audience claps and laughs as the interaction unfolds, with the stage backdrop displaying the 'Italia's Got Talent' logo in vibrant blue and purple lighting. The atmosphere is lively and playful, with the judges and audience clearly entertained by the dog's performance and the spontaneous interaction with Joel.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the brightly lit stage of *Italia's Got Talent*, featuring a large blue and purple star backdrop, dynamic lighting, and a reflective floor. Chico, a bald man in a yellow t-shirt, stands with his brown and white dog, Joe, who barks playfully as the audience laughs and applauds. Chico gives commands like "Chico left" and "Right" in English and Italian, prompting Joe to respond with barks and movements. Joel, a bald judge in a brown jacket, joins them on stage, holding a green ball. He engages Joe, asking in Italian, "Ok Joe, can you ask him where your shoes are in English?" and then directly to Joe, "Where are my shoes?", eliciting more barks and laughter. Joe performs a playful trick, jumping up and placing his paws on Joel’s leg, and then moves in a can-can-like motion as Chico encourages him to 'dance a little'. The judges, including a woman whose voice exclaims, "They can dance the can-can!", react with delight, clapping and smiling. On-screen text identifies the dog as 'CHICO 2 ANNI' and displays subtitles like 'OK Joe, would you ask him where your shoes are?' and 'They can dance the can-can! ;)', enhancing the comedic and informative tone of the lively performance.

segments.integrated_caption: On the brightly lit stage of *Italia's Got Talent*, featuring a glossy reflective floor and dynamic blue and purple lighting, Chico, a bald man in a yellow t-shirt, stands with a microphone, engaging in a playful challenge with Joel, a bald man in a brown jacket, who is accompanied by his brown and white dog, Joe. The segment opens with Chico's voice speaking in Italian, followed by Joel's affirmative response, amidst the sounds of a lively audience. Chico then instructs Joel to play hide-and-seek with Joe, giving him a green ball and directing him to count to ten while Joe hides. Chico's voice is heard giving detailed instructions, including demonstrating the 'Cucu' (peek-a-boo) call. As Joel follows the instructions, counting aloud in Italian, Joe energetically runs around the stage, eventually hiding behind a small white hurdle. The audience reacts with laughter and applause, while the judging panel, including a man in a checkered shirt and a woman in a patterned blouse, exclaims in Italian 'He's cheating!' ('Sta imbrogliando!') and 'Don't cheat!' ('Non barare!') in response to Joe’s quick movements. A female judge's voice is distinctly heard saying, 'Amore, guarda come conta!' (Love, look how he counts!). The stage's large backdrop displays the show’s logo, and on-screen graphics like '#IGT MERCOLEDÌ ALLE 21.30 SU 8' and subtitles such as 'OK, Joe. One last thing.', 'I'll have you play hide-and-seek with my dog.', 'He's counting!', 'Joe, you go hide yourself with the ball.', 'Don't cheat!', and 'He's cheating!' enhance the comedic and interactive tone. The segment ends with Chico asking Joel if he’s ready, setting up the next phase of the playful challenge, all while the audience continues to clap and cheer.

segments.integrated_caption: GENERATION_FAILED

The segment opens on the *Italia's Got Talent* stage, where Chico, a bald man in a yellow t-shirt and jeans, stands with his brown dog Joe, who actively performs a trick by balancing a white ball on his nose. A male judge, likely the man in the checkered shirt, expresses his amazement in Italian, stating he's "never seen anything like it," followed by applause and cheering. The judging panel reacts with amusement and praise, as Federica, the woman in the patterned blouse, jokes in Italian about giving her unruly dog Vanessa to Chico, acknowledging his skill. The bald man in the brown jacket, Joe, then shares his sentiment in Italian, stating that the more people he knows, the more he wants to stay with his dog, eliciting laughter from the audience. The voting begins with a judge calling out "Mara?", who confirms with "Sì," followed by "Fede?", to which Federica also responds "Sì." When "Joe?" is called, the bald man in the brown jacket comically barks "Woof woof!" and lets out a prolonged "Woo-hoo!" The man in the checkered shirt then exclaims in Italian, "And so Chico, it's four woofs!", as dog barks and enthusiastic audience cheers confirm the unanimous approval. Chico acknowledges the audience, as a judge notes "He thanked us!" and another offers "Congratulations!", amidst continued applause and cheering, with an English voice adding, "He's happy, right away. We're happy." The scene dramatically transitions to the *Canada's Got Talent* stage, marked by a shift from blue and white to vibrant red and gold lighting and set design, featuring a large red star and the show's logo. Alexandra Cote, a woman in a white blouse, is introduced with a graphic overlay, holding a calm, small white and gray speckled dog in a black vest with a gold star. A female voice welcomes them in English, saying "I love it already. Welcome!", met with applause.

The woman in the patterned blouse, later identified as Federica, jokes about giving her dog Vanessa to Chico, while the bald man in the brown jacket, Joe, expresses his preference for staying with his dog over people. The judges unanimously give their approval, with the man in the checkered shirt exclaiming, 'Then it's 4 'woofs' for Chico!' as the audience cheers. The scene then cuts to a different talent show, *Canada's Got Talent*, where Alexandra Cote, a woman in a white blouse, is introduced with a graphic overlay. She is seen holding a small white and gray speckled dog wearing a black vest with a gold star and red paw pads, which appears calm and content. The stage backdrop features a large red star and the show's logo, with dynamic lighting and a glossy floor reflecting the vibrant colors. The transition between the two shows is marked by a shift in lighting and set design, from the blue and white *Italia's Got Talent* stage to the red and gold *Canada's Got Talent* stage, highlighting the contrast in visual themes and performances.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on the brightly lit *Canada's Got Talent* stage, featuring a glossy reflective floor, dynamic red and gold lighting, and a large star-shaped backdrop with the show’s logo. Alexandra Côté, from Thetford Mines, QC, stands holding a microphone and a pink frisbee, accompanied by her small white and gray speckled dog, who wears a black vest with a gold star and red paw pads. A judge's voice asks, "Who are you?" to which Alexandra responds, "Alexandra Côté," and confirms she's "from Thetford Mines, Quebec." When asked about her dog, she introduces her as "Tesla," correcting the visual context's "Lilly." The judge, amused, makes a pun, "So she doesn't have gas," to which Alexandra replies, "Yes, she have energy." She further explains her motivation, stating, "dogs are my best friends," before a judge wishes her "good luck, the stage is yours." Alexandra thanks them as the audience erupts in applause and cheers. Energetic instrumental music with a distinct rhythm begins, and Tesla barks, prompting a judge to exclaim, "Turn it up!" Tesla then energetically runs across the stage, weaving around blue blocks and responding to Alexandra's expressive hand gestures, guided through a choreographed routine. The judging panel, including Trish in a white blazer, Howie in a pink jacket, and Kardinal in a denim jacket, watches intently from their desk, adorned with red buzzers and Tim Hortons cups. The audience, seated in a dimly lit theater, claps and cheers as Tesla performs tricks, including jumping over obstacles and running through a blue tunnel. As the music continues with lyrics, a judge excitedly comments, "Look what I'm missing!" The performance culminates with Tesla leaping onto Alexandra’s back as she spins, eliciting another "Wow!" from a judge and roaring applause from the audience. The stage lights flash in sync with Tesla’s movements, creating a vibrant atmosphere. On-screen graphics display Alexandra’s name and hometown. The segment ends with Alexandra smiling and waving to the audience, Tesla sitting calmly beside her, as the judges and audience continue their enthusiastic applause.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on the vibrant *Canada's Got Talent* stage, featuring a glossy black floor and a large illuminated red star backdrop with the show's logo, as upbeat electronic music pulses and the audience cheers enthusiastically. Alexandra Côté, in a black sequined top, guides her small white and gray speckled dog, Lilly, through an energetic agility routine. Lilly, wearing a black vest with a gold star, leaps over blue blocks and dashes through a tunnel with high energy, prompting an audible "Oh!" from the crowd and a male judge to exclaim "Great!" The judging panel—Howie, Lilly, Trish, and Kardinal—reacts with visible excitement, with Lilly clapping and Kardinal smiling broadly. The performance builds to a dynamic sequence where Lilly jumps over a blue block, landing gracefully to a standing ovation, intense cheering, and a female judge declaring "Yes!" followed by another female judge stating, "That was incredible!" The scene then transitions to the *Britain's Got Talent* stage, now bathed in dramatic blue and purple lights, accompanied by orchestral music. A male voice announces "Dave and Finn" as the man and his German Shepherd are introduced. The judges, including Simon, Amanda, Alesha, and David, are seated, and Amanda Holden begins to speak, her voice heard as the segment concludes.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a German Shepherd, Finn, panting with his tongue out, as a female voice wishes Dave "Good luck" and expresses excitement for surprises. The scene transitions to Dave, a man in a gray polo shirt and glasses, standing on a vibrant blue-lit stage with a large star backdrop and a digital London skyline reflected on the glossy floor. On-screen text identifies them as 'DAVE & FINN, AGED 42 & 9'. Dave speaks to the audience, stating, "Now we all think our dogs are magical, but Finn truly is, and for more than one reason," while Finn lies calmly beside him. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where Simon Cowell, in a pinstripe suit, listens intently before instructing Dave in a distinct voice, "David, I'd like you to think of an object and write that word down. Don't show me." Dave confirms with a "Yes" when asked if he's done it. Simon then directs Dave to "Hold it up to your chest. And come and join us at the front of the stage." As Dave walks toward the judges, engaging with one of them, Simon continues, "In a minute, I'm going to close my eyes. And when I do, I want you to show Finn the word." Finn remains lying down, occasionally looking up at Dave, while the audience reacts with smiles. Dave gestures toward Finn, who then stands up and sits attentively beside him. Simon reiterates, "Show him the word," and then asks Dave to "please return to your seat." The stage lighting remains dynamic with blue and purple hues, and the large star logo is prominently displayed as Simon concludes, "Now David, there's no possible way I could know what word you've picked," setting the stage for the act, all underscored by a gentle, building orchestral background music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a glossy stage with a vibrant blue star backdrop and a digital London skyline, where Dave, a man in a gray polo shirt and glasses, stands beside his German Shepherd, Finn, who is sitting calmly. Dave addresses Finn, saying "No," then "Finn," and asks, "What was that word?" as light, emotional background music begins to play. The camera cuts to the judging panel, showing Simon Cowell in a pinstripe suit holding a notepad, Amanda Holden in a red top, Alesha Dixon in a black leather jacket, and David Walliams in a dark suit, all observing intently. Dave then addresses David Walliams by name, stating, "David, the word is... Table." He then instructs, "Hold that up high so everyone can see," prompting an audible "Oh my god!" from Amanda Holden and a burst of enthusiastic audience applause and cheers. Dave continues, explaining, "Now everybody thinks that their dog is amazing, but Finn truly is." As emotional, orchestral music swells, Dave kneels beside Finn. A large screen behind them displays text: 'Finn may be a mind reader, but my connection with him goes far deeper.' This is followed by a photo of Dave in police uniform with Finn, and text revealing he is a policeman and Finn has been his partner for seven years. The screen then shows a dramatic moment: 'In 2016, whilst on Police duty, an offender went to stab me with a 10 inch knife. My life was in danger.' The emotional weight of the story is underscored by the judges’ serious expressions and the audience’s rapt attention, culminating in a powerful moment of connection between Dave and Finn, who remains calm and loyal throughout the segment, accompanied by the continuous emotional music.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a young girl speaking emotionally, though her words are not heard. The scene then transitions to a stage where Dave, a man in a gray polo shirt and glasses, kneels beside his German Shepherd, Finn, on a glossy stage with a large star backdrop and vibrant blue and purple lighting. As Dave sings a slow, emotional song with piano accompaniment, his voice expressing lyrics like "I feel so lost without you... I just feel crushed without you... I've been strong for so long, I never thought how much I needed you," on-screen text narrates a harrowing story. The text reads: 'Finn bravely jumped in front of me and saved my life. Whilst risking his own.' This is accompanied by images of Finn in critical condition, lying on a medical table with tubes and a vet attending to him, while the text continues: 'He was left fighting for his life.' The audience, including judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, react with visible emotion; Simon looks somber, Amanda appears moved, and David covers his face in distress, all against the backdrop of Dave's heartfelt singing. The story progresses with text stating 'Seeing my best friend on the brink of death just broke my heart,' accompanied by intimate photos of Dave and Finn, including one where Finn nuzzles Dave’s face. As the music transitions to a more uplifting instrumental piece with piano and strings, the vet team’s efforts are acknowledged with the text 'Thanks to the amazing team of vets, Finn's life was saved,' followed by images of Finn recovering, surrounded by get-well cards and gifts. The segment concludes with joyful scenes of Finn back to health, playing with children and riding a jet ski with Dave, as the text 'My best friend was back' appears. The judges are shown smiling and moved, with Simon visibly touched, and the stage lights shine brightly, emphasizing the emotional triumph, while growing applause from the audience fills the air.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a large screen displaying emotional photos of a German Shepherd, Finn, and his owner, Dave, including a dramatic image of Finn on a jet ski and another of him injured, accompanied by text overlays like 'Finn bravely jumped in front of me and saved my life' and 'He was left fighting for his life,' all set to a slow, emotional orchestral score. As the scene transitions to the Britain's Got Talent stage, the audience erupts in loud applause and cheers. Dave, a man in a gray polo shirt and glasses, walks onto the stage with Finn, a large brown and black German Shepherd, who sits calmly beside him. An announcer with a British accent invites Simon Cowell to join them on stage, prompting another wave of enthusiastic applause and cheering from the visibly moved audience. The announcer then instructs Simon to kneel down and face Finn. The judges, including Simon, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, react with deep emotion, with Simon covering his face and Amanda appearing tearful. Dave, speaking with a British accent, explains that for the last two weeks, they've been asking friends for words to describe Finn, who barks softly twice during Dave's explanation. Dave then lists these words with emphasis: 'companion,' 'brave,' 'amazing,' 'incredible,' and 'fast.' He concludes by asking Simon to hold his left hand in a specific way, as the emotional music continues softly in the background, highlighting the powerful and heartfelt nature of the performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment begins with a close-up of Finn, a German Shepherd, panting calmly on stage. A male voice, identified as Dave, instructs, "Now I want you to look into Finn's eyes. Keep looking into Finn's eyes, and with your right thumb, I want you to open a page. Have a peek at that word and remember it." The scene then shows Simon Cowell, holding a red buzzer with a thoughtful expression, followed by David Walliams gesturing as he speaks, and Amanda Holden listening intently in a striking red dress. Dave then asks Simon, "Now Simon, for the first time, can you tell us what that word was?" to which Simon replies, "Heroic." Dave explains, "Now there's no possible way I could know what word you were going to pick," with Simon interjecting, "No." Dave continues, "But Finn did. Because before we came here this evening, Finn had a word engraved on the tag on his collar." A wide shot reveals Dave kneeling beside Finn on the glossy stage, with David Walliams standing nearby, as Dave asks Simon to "please have a look at the tag on Finn's collar and read out loud the word that's there." Simon reads, "Heroic," prompting Dave to exclaim, "Heroic!" as the audience erupts in cheers and applause. Simon Cowell is then seen kneeling beside Dave and Finn, engaging with the dog, while the judges' panel, with names displayed, and the illuminated 'Britain's Got Talent' logo are visible. The audience reacts emotionally, some covering their faces, as uplifting, emotional music with a female vocalist begins. The segment concludes with a joyful close-up of Finn looking up, bathed in the stage lights.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with the judging panel—Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams—clapping enthusiastically from their seats, their names displayed above them on illuminated blue signs, as upbeat, dramatic music with a choir-like sound plays and the audience cheers. The brightly lit stage, featuring dynamic blue and red lighting, a large star-shaped backdrop, and a glossy reflective floor, transitions to a more energetic, slightly circus-like tune as a man in a dark suit with a teal tie, holding a clipboard labeled 'Guinness World Records Officially Amazing,' stands center stage. He introduces himself with a clear, authoritative voice as Alan Pixley, an official Guinness World Records adjudicator, to loud audience cheers. The camera cuts to Mitch, a man in a red and blue tracksuit, kneeling beside a small, fluffy dog named Cally, who wears a Union Jack bandana and is positioned on a red mat with a blue tarp draped over a large object. Alan Pixley, speaking with a serious yet excited demeanor, announces that "Mitch and Cally the Wonderdog" will attempt the Guinness World Records title for the "fastest time to pop 100 balloons by a dog," eliciting "oohs" and cheers from the audience. He continues to explain the rules: "time will start as soon as Cally first her first balloon, and Cally can burst the balloons with any part of her body." The stage is framed by red and white striped bollards, and the backdrop displays a large, illuminated Union Jack, while on-screen graphics display 'TOP TALENT' and 'MITCH AND CALLY'. Alan Pixley concludes by stating the current record to beat is "44.49 seconds" held by an American dog named Anastasia, as the audience murmurs and claps. The camera zooms in on Cally, highlighting her fluffy coat and Union Jack bandana, as Mitch gently pets her. Alan Pixley raises his arms in approval as Cally performs a trick, leaping over a small obstacle, with the judges reacting with visible excitement, clapping and leaning forward, fully engaged in the electric atmosphere of the performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an announcer's voice declaring a 'Guinness World Records title' attempt, counting down 'Three, two, one, go!' as a close-up shows Cally, a small, fluffy light-brown dog in a Union Jack bandana, being guided by Mitch, a man in a red and blue tracksuit. As the 'go' command is given, intense, fast-paced electronic music with a strong beat begins, and Cally leaps over a small obstacle onto a brightly lit stage with a large Union Jack backdrop and a glossy reflective floor. She energetically runs through a red mat filled with red and blue balloons, popping them with distinct popping sounds, creating a burst of colorful fragments. Mitch's voice, a male British accent, is heard constantly encouraging her with shouts of 'Go, go, go!', 'Come on, Cally, come on!', and 'Yes, yes, yes, come on, come on!' The camera alternates between close-ups of Cally's energetic movements and wide shots of Mitch encouraging her from the side. As the balloons are quickly reduced to scattered pieces, Cally continues to run around the now clearer mat. The scene transitions to a wide view of the full Britain's Got Talent set, revealing the judging panel—Simon, Amanda, Alesha, and David—visibly delighted and clapping. The large screen behind the stage displays the number '42'. The music fades, replaced by a crescendo of loud applause, cheers, and whistles from the audience. The judges stand and cheer enthusiastically, with Simon Cowell smiling broadly, Amanda Holden clapping with excitement, Alesha Dixon laughing, and David Walliams raising his arms in approval, their voices heard exclaiming 'Yes!', 'Wow!', and 'Amazing!' amidst the celebratory atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The Britain's Got Talent stage is a vibrant scene of celebration, bathed in dynamic red, white, and blue lighting with a 'WORLD RECORD' backdrop, fireworks, and confetti. The air is filled with the sounds of balloons popping and a wildly cheering audience as Mitch, in his red and blue tracksuit, guides Cally, the small fluffy light-brown dog wearing a Union Jack bandana, who has just completed a record-breaking balloon-popping challenge. A Guinness World Records official, a bearded man in a dark suit with a teal tie, stands center stage, holding his 'Officially Amazing' clipboard. He builds suspense, repeatedly exclaiming "Hang on!" before asking, "Alan, can you confirm for us, was that a new Guinness World Record?" Another voice, identified as Alan, confirms, "I confirm it was a valid attempt. And Cally the Wonder Dog scored 41.67 seconds, which means she's a new Guinness World Record holder!" This announcement unleashes a torrent of celebratory music, intensified cheers, and more balloon pops. Confetti rains down as Mitch receives a large championship-style belt, and Cally joyfully runs across the red mat, popping any remaining balloons. The Judging Panel—Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams—are on their feet, applauding enthusiastically, their names displayed on surrounding digital screens. The official exclaims, "You did it! Cally, you did it!" followed by collective "Congratulations! Well done!" from the judges and official. The official then turns to the judges, stating, "Simon, we have to start with you, you're down for the act, and we've got a new Guinness World Record holder!" Simon Cowell, visibly emotional, responds with a distinct voice, "I'm so proud of what she did. She's a world..." before the audio fades, encapsulating a moment of pure triumph and joy.

The stage is illuminated with dynamic red, white, and blue lighting, and the backdrop prominently displays the words 'WORLD RECORD' in large metallic letters, with fireworks and confetti effects enhancing the festive atmosphere. The Judging Panel, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, is seen standing and applauding enthusiastically from their desk, which features red buzzers and illuminated 'X' designs. Center stage, Mitch, wearing a red and blue tracksuit with a Union Jack patch, holds Cally, a small fluffy light-brown dog wearing a Union Jack bandana, who has just completed a balloon-popping challenge. A man in a dark suit with a teal tie, identified as a Guinness World Records official, stands beside them, holding a clipboard labeled 'Guinness World Records Officially Amazing' and speaking with excitement. Confetti rains down as Mitch receives a large championship-style belt from the official, and the dog Cally runs freely across the red mat, popping remaining balloons. The audience is shown cheering wildly, and the judges are visibly emotional, with Simon Cowell clapping and smiling broadly. The stage is surrounded by large digital screens displaying the names of the judges—SIMON, AMANDA, ALESHA, DAVID—above their respective seats, reinforcing the show's branding. The overall mood is triumphant and joyous, with the entire set bathed in celebratory lights and effects.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an unseen announcer's voice setting the stage for a 'balloon popping competition,' mentioning a 'world record holder's daughter' who might challenge Cally. Simon Cowell, dressed in a white shirt, reacts with surprise and amusement, audibly exclaiming 'Really?' and then praising the performance as 'amazing' and Cally as 'adorable.' The scene quickly shifts to Cally, a small, fluffy light-brown dog wearing a Union Jack bandana, energetically running and popping red and blue balloons on a glossy stage with a large star backdrop and confetti scattered across the floor. Mitch, a man in a red and blue tracksuit with a Union Jack patch, stands beside a red mat, holding a championship belt, guiding Cally through the balloon-popping challenge with expressive hand gestures. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where David Walliams, in a dark suit, claps enthusiastically, audibly stating 'we've witnessed history tonight' and finding it 'strangely really compelling to watch a dog burst balloons,' even expressing disappointment when it was over. Alesha Dixon, in a black leather outfit, and Amanda Holden, in a red dress, react with visible excitement, with Alesha exclaiming, 'That was just so exciting and that is why I just love this show, because of moments like that. Cally, you are a legend!' Amanda adds, 'Great dog!' and describes the act as 'completely barking mad,' expressing anticipation to see the 'huge belt on that dog.' The stage is illuminated with dynamic blue and red lighting, and the atmosphere is celebratory, with the audience's applause audible throughout. The video alternates between close-ups of Cally’s joyful expressions and wide shots of the stage, capturing the audience’s applause and the judges’ reactions. The performance concludes with Mitch and Cally standing proudly on stage, surrounded by confetti, as the judges continue to cheer and applaud, with Alesha interjecting 'Yeah!'

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of a red championship belt, adorned with a Union Jack and sparkling details, held by Mitch, a man in a red and blue tracksuit with a Union Jack patch. He stands proudly on a stage with a red mat, ropes, and confetti, holding the belt and a small, fluffy light-brown dog named Cally, who wears a Union Jack bandana. Two men in suits, likely hosts, stand beside him. A female host praises Cally and Mitch, stating they were 'part of television history tonight,' did a 'great job,' and commends their 'training.' Another male host adds, 'Well done Mitch, well done Cally.' The female host notes Cally 'can't wait to get in there and get all the balloons,' with a male host calling her 'savage.' Cally energetically runs across the stage, popping red and blue balloons with her body, encouraged by Mitch's hand gestures. A male host jokes, 'Don't try this at home,' which another host emphatically repeats, 'You absolutely can't try that at home,' referring to Cally's balloon-popping prowess. They confirm Cally 'absolutely loves' popping balloons. The hosts continue to congratulate them, calling Mitch and Cally 'the dynamic duo, your favorite.' The scene transitions to a different stage with a large golden star backdrop, where Claudiu Popa, in a gray sweatshirt and black pants, walks on with a black dog, likely a Doberman, on a leash. The dog performs a trick, jumping to place its paws on the judge’s desk, occupied by a woman in a black sequined outfit with star embellishments, whose desk also holds a basket of red roses and a Tymbark product. The stage is dimly lit with blue and gold lighting, with the audience visible. Judges, including a man in a brown jacket with a beard and a man in a checkered shirt, watch intently. A slow, emotional female-vocal pop song with piano and strings begins to play, with lyrics like 'You're my sunshine, just me and you, light it up on the rise, let's make it tonight. You'll never be alone, I'll be with you from dusk till dawn,' as Claudiu and the dog walk across the stage, the dog’s tail wagging, with a faint dog bark heard during the music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman in a black sequined outfit with star embellishments, seated at a judging panel, smiling and gesturing with a pen in hand, her desk adorned with a red buzzer, a Tymbark product box, and a basket of red roses. As a female voice sings a melodic tune with background music, the scene transitions to a vibrant stage, illuminated with dynamic blue and purple lighting, featuring a large, ornate golden star backdrop and a glossy reflective floor. Claudiu Popa, a man in a gray sweatshirt with a red number tag and black pants, walks onto the stage with his black Doberman-like dog on a leash. A male voice comments in Romanian, noting how the dog "dances when it walks," while another male voice emphasizes that "what he does here are training things, effectively training, it's less of a show," to which a female voice agrees. The energetic dog circles Claudiu, jumping up to place its paws on his legs and then playfully on the judge’s desk. Claudiu skillfully guides the dog through a series of precise tricks, including standing on its hind legs and jumping over a small wicker box, all performed with high energy and responsiveness to his hand signals. The audience is visible in the background, clapping and reacting with excitement, while the background music and singing continue. Amidst the performance, a female voice exclaims "Oh, Doamne!" (Oh, God!) twice, expressing surprise or delight. A male voice further explains that the dog "understands from signs practically; it's training without words." The routine culminates in a synchronized moment where the dog stands on its hind legs, reaching up to Claudiu’s hand, showcasing their strong bond and impressive training.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an excited audience member clapping under vibrant blue stage lighting, as an upbeat pop song with female vocals and lyrics like "You'll never be alone, I'll be with you from dusk till dawn" plays. The music then fades, replaced by rising audience applause and murmuring as Claudiu Popa, in a gray sweatshirt and black pants, walks his sleek black dog, Eli, on a leash across a dark, glossy stage featuring a starry backdrop. Eli performs a trick by placing its paws on a wicker box, prompting Claudiu to lift a sign that reads 'Fericirea începe cu un nas umed. Şi se termină cu o coadă' (Happiness begins with a wet nose. And ends with a tail). A male narrator's voice in Romanian explains, "They understood each other through signs, although Eli certainly knows Romanian. They are tied to each other, communicate, work together and have..." The audience erupts in applause as Eli runs toward the judges' panel. A woman in a black sequined outfit with star embellishments, seated at the panel, warmly pets Eli, exclaiming with an American accent, "Oh my goodness! I love it! I swear!" Another judge, a man with a beard in a brown jacket, smiles and applauds, adding a clear "da" (yes) in Romanian, while a third male judge also reacts positively. Eli then joyfully runs across the dynamically lit stage, leaping over a small obstacle, as the camera captures the judges' enthusiastic responses, including the female judge playfully remarking, "He looks like Barry Manilow!" The stage is illuminated with blue and purple lights, featuring a large golden star and ornate red and gold patterns. The audience continues to cheer loudly, and the judges’ desk displays sponsor products like Tymbark dog treats and a basket of red roses. The segment concludes with Eli running happily across the stage, tail wagging, as the judges and audience celebrate the performance, accompanied by a brief, theatrical musical flourish.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a vibrant 'Britain's Got Talent' stage, illuminated with dynamic blue and purple lighting and featuring the show's logo backdrop. Louise, in a sparkly maroon dress, walks her fluffy brown dog, Jasper, on a red leash, immediately beginning to sing in a high-pitched, operatic style. Jasper trots confidently across the glossy stage, occasionally looking up at Louise, who gestures and smiles. The audience is seen smiling and clapping, their laughter joining the soft piano music played by a pianist at a grand piano. A male judge comments, "He doesn't want to sing," and later, "He doesn't want to do anything." Louise kneels, encouraging Jasper, saying, "Oh Jasper, give us a chew. Just one woof." The judges, including a man in a brown jacket, a woman in a yellow blazer, and a man in a dark suit, react with amusement and laughter. A male judge notes, "He does not like that," as Jasper emits high-pitched yelps and whines. Another judge exclaims, "He woofs out," followed by the observation, "Never has a dog wanted to leave the stage more," as Jasper continues to vocalize with a mix of whines, howls, and distinct woofs, often accompanying Louise's ongoing operatic performance. As Louise stands and raises her arm, the judges continue to laugh and applaud, with the man in the dark suit standing to cheer as Jasper walks toward their desk. The segment concludes with close-ups of Jasper’s expressive face and the judges’ delighted reactions, as a judge remarks, "I think he wasn't feeling it today," capturing a lighthearted and joyful moment.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Simon Cowell, seated at the judging panel, reacting with a serious expression as a large, fluffy brown dog, Jasper, runs past him. Over a slow, emotional piano track, Simon critically remarks, "Don't think he likes your singing very much," with another male judge agreeing, "It's true." The camera cuts to Louise, in a sparkly maroon dress, walking confidently across the stage with Jasper on a leash. The stage is illuminated with vibrant blue and purple neon lights, forming a dynamic geometric backdrop, with a grand piano visible. The audience claps and smiles, clearly engaged. The judges are shown, with Simon appearing thoughtful and Amanda Holden smiling warmly. Simon questions, "Why do it?" and another male judge declares, "So I'm gonna say no," before Simon dismissively adds, "Oh, shut up." Louise and Jasper walk calmly across the stage, Jasper occasionally looking up at her. Amanda laughs, Alesha Dixon appears amused, and Alesha apologetically states, "I'm so sorry." Amanda then says, "I think today I'm gonna say no." Simon explains, "Yeah, and we've got to remember we're looking for an act that can go on live television, that can sing for the Royal Family," with Amanda interjecting, "It's gonna be great on live television," followed by laughter. Amanda asks, "So are you saying no, David?" and a male judge confirms, "Yeah, I'm gonna say no, actually," then adds, "Nice to meet you all." Amanda comments, "I can see why he didn't say anything. There's a lot of pressure on him." The scene shifts to a wider shot of the stage, showing the full panel with their red buzzers and illuminated 'X' symbols. The background music dramatically changes to an upbeat, theatrical show-tune, accompanied by enthusiastic audience cheering, with a voice singing, "This is the greatest show!" Amanda exclaims, "Here we go!" amidst laughter. The video then cuts to a different act: Michelle, in a sparkly silver dress, holding a small, brown and tan dachshund wearing a Union Jack bow tie, standing on stage with a microphone. The judges react with surprise and amusement, Simon raising his hands in astonishment. Amanda greets Michelle, "Hello darling, how are you?" Michelle responds, "I'm good, thank you. How are you?" Amanda asks, "I'm great. What's your name?" Michelle replies, "My name's Michelle." Amanda inquires, "And who's your lovely friend?" Michelle introduces, "This is my best friend Mouse." Amanda, surprised, repeats, "Mouse?" followed by laughter. The audience claps and laughs. On-screen graphics display 'TOP TALENT' and 'MICHELLE AND MOUSE'. Amanda then asks Michelle about her "big dream," as the final shot shows the full stage with the 'BRITAIN'S GOT TALENT' logo prominently displayed, maintaining a lively and energetic atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on a brightly lit stage, adorned with dynamic blue and purple lights and a large star backdrop featuring the 'Britain's Got Talent' logo. On-screen graphics introduce 'MICHELLE & MOUSE' and 'DACHSHUND, 5'. Michelle, in a sparkly silver dress, kneels on a Union Jack-patterned mat, gently holding her dachshund, Mouse, who wears a Union Jack bow tie. As Mouse barks into a microphone, Michelle smiles warmly. The judging panel, including Simon Cowell in a sequined jacket, Amanda Holden in a gray blazer, Alesha Dixon in a black leather outfit, and Bruno Tonioli, react with surprise and amusement, Simon gesturing animatedly. Amanda's voice is heard asking Michelle about Mouse being a model, to which Michelle confirms, 'Yeah,' prompting Simon to joke, 'Oh, he's like Kate Moss!' Amidst laughter, Amanda then asks what Michelle would do with the prize money, and Michelle humorously replies that Mouse 'would be using it to get himself a pair of new hips,' drawing more laughter. Amanda adds she'd probably do the same. Simon then predicts, 'I'm guessing he's gonna sing,' a sentiment echoed by Amanda and Alesha. As upbeat, dramatic music begins, Mouse starts barking rhythmically, mimicking a song. Close-ups show the dog's performance and the judges' reactions, with Bruno laughing and pointing. Amanda exclaims, 'What song?' and Alesha asks, 'This is your song, is it?' Simon identifies it as 'an Alicia song' as Mouse continues to bark, mimicking specific vocal runs and lyrics like 'I'm running out of patience 'cause I can't believe...' and later, 'I love you too much, it shows all my emotions.' Simon humorously adds, 'To be fair, I do that when Alicia comes on the radio,' and later, 'Backing vocals.' The audience is visibly engaged, clapping and smiling throughout the comedic performance. After his act, Mouse is handed to a man in a black shirt and taken backstage, while the judges continue to react with laughter and applause, maintaining the lively and humorous atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with an upbeat, quirky pop song featuring a female vocalist, as the Judging Panel—Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and Bruno Tonioli—reacts with laughter and surprise. The focus shifts to Michelle, a woman in a sparkly silver dress, kneeling on a vibrantly lit stage with a large star backdrop and glossy reflective floor. Her small, brown and tan dachshund, wearing a Union Jack bow tie, barks enthusiastically into a microphone, prompting Simon Cowell to lean forward in astonishment and remark, "Maybe it's a cry for help." Bruno Tonioli laughs heartily, and a male voice from the panel shouts, "Turn it off!" in amusement amidst the dog's continued barking and the pop music. The dog then affectionately licks Michelle’s face, drawing more laughter from the visibly engaged audience, who are clapping and smiling, and the judges. After the dog is handed off backstage, the judges continue their reactions; Alesha Dixon covers her mouth in delight, and Amanda Holden claps. Bruno exclaims, "Well done, mate!" Amanda adds, "Oh!" Alesha questions the music choice, "Why play it slow?" to which Amanda replies, "I don't know," and Alesha confirms, "She chose it herself!" Simon Cowell then offers his feedback, stating, "Sometimes you hear a great song and you just think, you know, it can't be better. But actually, that was better than the original, in my opinion," adding, "And I'm being serious, and I don't say that very often," to audience laughter. He later suggests, "If you did go forward, then maybe Mouse and Alicia could perform together," possibly referring to the dog as "Mouse." Amanda responds, "It would be different. I'd... I like a challenge," before Bruno concludes, "I almost pressed that," indicating his positive reception. The lively and humorous atmosphere is underscored by the dog’s energetic performance and the judges’ exaggerated, vocal reactions.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the Britain's Got Talent stage with Michelle, in a sparkly silver top, alongside her dachshund, now identified as 'Mouse,' who sports a red, white, and blue Union Jack bow tie. Mouse barks into a microphone, drawing laughter and animated gestures from the judging panel, which includes Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and Bruno Tonioli. Despite an initial visual of buzzers lit, the judges' audio reveals a positive outcome: Amanda finds the act 'very charming, very unexpected' and gives a 'yes,' followed by Alesha's 'yes,' and Bruno's 'yes to Mouse.' Simon then confirms, 'Mouse, you've got four yeses,' as energetic, celebratory music swells with crowd cheering and a female voice singing. Amanda exclaims, 'That was amazing! That was hilarious!' and Simon jokes about Mouse doing an album or a remix. Michelle, initially appearing disappointed, walks off stage with Mouse, who is then handed off backstage by a man in a black shirt. The scene transitions to Japan's Got Talent, where Tetsuro, in a plaid shirt and brown scarf, calmly introduces himself in Japanese as 'Ito Tetsuro' to an announcer, who asks about his upcoming performance on the vibrant, neon-lit stage.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the vibrant stage of *Japan's Got Talent*, illuminated with dynamic blue and pink neon lights and a large star-shaped backdrop. Tetsuro (46), in a plaid shirt and brown pants, stands center stage, his calm voice introducing himself and asking if he can call his partner. A judge's voice grants permission, and Tetsuro calls out, "Sunny, come!" A black and white Border Collie with a red collar, Sunny, joyfully runs to his side, eliciting enthusiastic cheers and applause from the attentive audience and judges. Tetsuro then introduces Sunny as his partner, explaining in his calm voice that they will perform a "dog dance" that expresses their daily life story since meeting, promising a fun and happy narrative. Judges acknowledge his explanation with "I see" and "Understood." As soft, emotional background music begins, Tetsuro gestures to a cardboard doghouse prop on stage, which symbolizes the cold, rainy winter night he met Sunny. His voice shifts to a heartfelt narrative, recounting the story: "You're cute... Are you all alone? I'm all alone too. Then, let's live together." This touching backstory, accompanied by the music, draws laughter and "aww"s from the audience and judges, with one judge commenting, "Ah, cute." The performance then begins with Tetsuro guiding Sunny through playful leaps and synchronized turns, the dog wagging its tail excitedly, as the stage lighting subtly shifts to enhance the emotional tone.
segments.visual_original_caption: The segment opens on the vibrant stage of *Japan's Got Talent*, illuminated with dynamic blue and pink neon lights and a large star-shaped backdrop. A man, identified as Tetsuro (age 46) via on-screen text, stands center stage in a plaid shirt and brown pants, introducing his dog, Sunny, a black and white Border Collie with a red collar. He calls Sunny to him, and the dog joyfully runs to his side. Tetsuro shares a heartfelt story of meeting Sunny on a cold, rainy winter night, symbolized by a cardboard doghouse prop on stage. He gestures to the doghouse, then to Sunny, who emerges from it, and they interact warmly. The stage features a glossy reflective floor, and the judges—Hamada, Alice, Gackt, and Yamada—are seated at a long desk with their names displayed above. Tetsuro explains they will perform a dance and share their daily life story, promising a fun and happy narrative. The audience watches attentively, and the atmosphere is warm and emotional, underscored by the bond between Tetsuro and Sunny. The performance begins with Tetsuro guiding Sunny through a series of movements, including a playful leap and a synchronized turn, as the dog wags its tail excitedly. The stage lighting shifts subtly to match the mood, enhancing the emotional tone of the act.

segments.integrated_caption: On the vibrant *Japan's Got Talent* stage, illuminated by dynamic blue and pink neon lights and a glossy reflective floor, an upbeat, slightly whimsical background music plays as Tetsuro, a man in a plaid shirt and brown pants, begins his emotional act. He is seated on a stool beside a small table, where his black and white Border Collie, Sunny, stands on a matching stool, playfully interacting with a cup and straw. Tetsuro's calm, emotional Japanese narration begins, accompanied by on-screen subtitles stating, 'When Sunny came to my house, every day began to sparkle.' The audience claps, and the music becomes more tender as Tetsuro lies down on the floor, and Sunny gently nudges him, showcasing their deep bond. Sunny then runs around a beige cardboard doghouse labeled 'THANK YOU,' symbolizing their story of meeting. Tetsuro's narration continues, stating in Japanese that 'Life with Sunny was fulfilling,' followed by a magical chime sound effect. The music shifts to a more melancholic, piano-driven melody as Tetsuro's voice becomes reflective and somber, narrating, 'But then, a year later...' and 'Perhaps I was a little too dependent on the always gentle Sunny.' Subtitles appear: 'Work got busier then less, and less time for fun,' as Tetsuro continues his narration, 'Work got busier, and fun time decreased...' He gestures dramatically, reflecting on changing dynamics, narrating, 'And I never thought this would happen.' The melancholic music persists as Tetsuro speaks the final quote, 'Someone once said, happiness is not something you look for,' with the music swelling slightly, while Sunny runs beside him, reinforcing the emotional core of their journey. The audience and judges, including a woman in a floral blouse, are visibly moved by the heartfelt storytelling and synchronized performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens on the vibrant stage of *Japan's Got Talent*, illuminated with dynamic blue and pink neon lights and a glossy reflective floor mirroring a star-shaped backdrop, as dramatic, emotional music plays. Tetsuro, a man in a plaid shirt and brown pants, stands center stage with his black and white Border Collie, Sunny, who emerges from a beige cardboard doghouse labeled 'THANK YOU'. Tetsuro, with a calm and emotional demeanor, kneels to embrace Sunny, whispering affectionately, "Sunny, we'll always be together," a tender moment underscored by his earlier voiceover stating, "I understood the meaning painfully well." Close-ups capture Sunny’s expressive face and Tetsuro’s tender gestures, as applause and cheers erupt from the dimly lit audience. The scene shifts to the judging panel—Hamada, Alice, Gackt, and Yamada—who react with visible emotion and laughter. A male judge, identified as Yamada in the visual context, comments on Sunny's performance, noting, "Well, you know, it's an animal. There are parts where it doesn't quite listen to what you say, but..." Another male judge adds, "But I think it moved so well up to that point," with Yamada concluding, "It was a fun feeling, wasn't it? It was cute," to which Alice responds with laughter. Yamada then transitions to feedback, stating that Sunny is the first animal act, "Up until now, it's all been people," a point Alice confirms, "Everyone's been human." He expresses a desire to see more of Sunny's act in the final, highlighting the poignant storytelling and the strong bond between Tetsuro and Sunny. The stage remains bathed in electric blue and purple hues, with the *Japan's Got Talent* logo prominently displayed above.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Sunny, a black and white Border Collie with a red collar, playfully sticking its tongue out and looking up against a vibrant, abstract blue and pink background. The scene transitions to Tetsuro, a man in a plaid shirt and brown pants, standing on the *Japan's Got Talent* stage with a large, illuminated star backdrop and dynamic blue and pink neon lighting. He speaks emotionally, introducing his dog and confirming its name is "Yes" (Iesu), as the audience erupts in applause and cheers. The camera cuts to the judging panel, where a woman with short, layered hair, wearing a black blazer over a white top and a silver choker, speaks with admiration. She notes her experience with six dogs, expressing surprise that "Yes" could perform so well in front of such a large audience, and humorously describes the dog's unique vocalization as a "dirty voice." Another voice, likely Tetsuro's, lightheartedly interjects, "You don't have to say it's dirty," to which Tetsuro confirms, "It's Yes," and thanks them. The audience is shown reacting with visible excitement and continued applause. The focus returns to Sunny, who stands calmly beside Tetsuro, panting happily, as another judge, a woman in a floral blouse, shares her own experience with two puppies, expressing how cute and impressive the act was and referencing a fellow judge's comment about the dog being "cunning." Tetsuro again confirms, "It's Yes," and thanks them amidst more applause. The final judge, a man with long hair tied back, wearing a black vest over a white shirt, gestures expansively, commenting on how cute the dog is and expressing a desire to see more. Throughout, the stage remains brightly lit with a glossy reflective floor, and the *Japan's Got Talent* logo is prominently displayed in the background, accompanied by continuous audience applause and murmurs of excitement.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Man in Black Vest and White Shirt, a judge, seated at the panel, gesturing expansively as he speaks in Japanese about the glamour of the occasion and his hope for a flashy performance, with a green bottle on the desk. The scene transitions to the *Japan's Got Talent* stage, where Tetsuro, a man in a plaid shirt and brown pants, stands with his black and white Border Collie, Sunny, who is wearing a red collar. Tetsuro thanks the judges in Japanese, and Sunny performs an energetic routine, including jumping and weaving around Tetsuro, who guides him with hand signals. The dog’s performance is met with enthusiastic audience applause and cheers. The Woman in White Top with Microphone exclaims "Congratulations!" in Japanese, and the Man in Black Vest and White Shirt announces in Japanese, "Congratulations! You have earned the right to advance to the finals!" On-screen text confirms, 'You have earned the advance to the finals!' The camera follows Sunny as he runs offstage, where he is greeted by crew members and judges, including the Man in Black Vest and White Shirt, who congratulates him again. Tetsuro expresses his happiness and relief that Sunny performed well despite his initial worries about performing in front of the audience, to which the judge praises Sunny for doing "very well." The scene shifts to a new act, accompanied by upbeat, dramatic music. Manny, a woman in a purple tank top and patterned leggings, enters the stage holding a small, fluffy white dog. She introduces herself in English as "Mahani" and her dog as "Robbie," identifying him as a Maltese Terrier, in response to a judge's questions. She then performs a dance routine with Robbie held securely in her arms, and the audience reacts with visible delight, their applause and cheers heard. The judges, including the Woman in White Top with Microphone, are shown clapping and smiling. The stage backdrop features a vibrant city skyline with dynamic blue and purple lighting, and the *Japan's Got Talent* logo is prominently displayed. The segment ends with Manny and her dog receiving applause, and the on-screen graphic identifies them as 'MANNY AND ROBBIE' under the 'TOP TALENT' banner.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Manny Djahanguiri, a woman with short blonde hair, wearing a purple tank top and patterned leggings, holding a small, fluffy white dog named Robbie. A male voice (David Walliams) asks, "what are you going to do today?", to which Manny replies, "Doga, I teach Doga." The scene quickly shifts to a wide shot of the judging panel, where four judges—Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams—are seated behind a desk. Simon exclaims "Doga!" in surprise, followed by Amanda asking, "Oh, what's that?" Manny explains, "Doga is yoga for you and your dog," prompting Alesha to say "Wicked" and the audience to react with a collective "Ohh." Simon then instructs, "You gotta watch the act. That's a good boy," and David adds, "Robbie, watch the act." Simon concludes, "I've got to see this," and Amanda responds, "Right, okay." Upbeat, suspenseful background music begins as the camera cuts to a wide view of the stage, showcasing a glossy reflective floor with a large star at center, set against a vibrant digital backdrop of London’s skyline. The audience is shown clapping and cheering under dynamic stage lighting. Manny then begins her performance, performing a yoga-inspired dance routine with Robbie on a purple mat placed over the star. The music shifts to a serene, ethereal tone with a female vocalization as Manny transitions into a seated pose, balancing her leg behind her head while holding Robbie securely in her lap, demonstrating impressive flexibility. David Walliams exclaims "Wow." Manny's calm voice guides the routine, saying "Breathe deeply," "In and out through your nose," and "Connect with your dog." The judges react with visible amazement—Simon Cowell covers his mouth in surprise, Amanda Holden smiles broadly, and David Walliams leans forward in awe. The camera alternates between Manny’s fluid movements and close-ups of the judges’ reactions, capturing the audience’s enthusiastic applause. On-screen graphics identify Manny as 'Manny Djahanguiri, Doga Teacher, 43,' confirming her role and age. The performance continues with Manny maintaining her balance and poise, while Robbie remains calm and attentive, occasionally looking around. Manny concludes her spoken guidance with "And bow." The stage lighting remains vibrant, with beams of blue and white light enhancing the spectacle. The segment concludes with Manny holding a serene pose, hands in prayer position, as the audience continues to cheer, and the judges remain visibly impressed, with Alesha Dixon smiling warmly and David Walliams clapping enthusiastically, all set to the continuing ethereal music and chanting.

The scene then shifts to the stage, where Manny Djahanguiri, a woman in a purple tank top and patterned leggings, performs a dynamic yoga-inspired dance routine with her small, fluffy white dog Robbie. She begins by sitting on a purple mat over the star on the stage, holding Robbie securely as she executes a series of flexible movements. Manny's calm voice instructs, "Place your dog on the top of your thighs," prompting a playful interjection from David Walliams, "I can't place my dog on my thighs, he's the size of them!" to audience laughter. Manny continues, guiding the audience to "rise up... into boat," explaining, "Your dog is the sailor, and you're the vessel," as the music swells and audience applause begins to build. She balances her leg behind her head while maintaining control of Robbie, who is heard panting softly. The backdrop features a vibrant digital display of London’s skyline, illuminated with blue and purple lighting. Manny announces they will "finish in hot dog," then repeats, "Place your dog on the top of your thighs," which elicits a confused "What are you doing?" from Simon Cowell and "What is she doing, Simon?" from David Walliams, followed by more audience laughter. As Manny transitions into a split, still holding Robbie, and then rolls onto her back, keeping the dog safe and calm, she instructs to "squeeze." The judges, including Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, and David Walliams, react with visible excitement, clapping and leaning forward in amazement, while the audience cheers and claps enthusiastically. The performance culminates in a dramatic pose where Manny lies on her back with Robbie nestled between her legs, both appearing relaxed and in sync. Manny triumphantly declares "Nama-paw!" to thunderous applause and cheers, then thanks everyone. The judges’ reactions range from stunned silence to loud applause, with Simon Cowell covering his mouth in astonishment, exclaiming, "Look how relaxed he is!" David Walliams adds, "Oh, Robbie!" and Simon echoes, "Robbie." David asks, "Do you like that?" and Simon responds, "That's good," with David concluding, "That's dog." The stage lighting remains dynamic, enhancing the visual spectacle. The video ends with a wide shot of the stage, showcasing the full performance and the judges’ reactions, emphasizing the emotional and physical connection between Manny and Robbie, as Simon Cowell speaks quickly and somewhat flustered, and David Walliams interjects.

segments.integrated_caption: The Britain's Got Talent stage glows with dynamic blue and purple lights against a London skyline backdrop, as the audience's cheers and applause fill the air. Manny Djahanguiri, a Doga Teacher, stands with her small, fluffy white dog Robbie, while a man kneels beside a black dog. Simon Cowell greets them with "Hello!" and "Good boy!", and David Walliams observes, "They've all got their dogs as well." Manny's voice is heard inviting, "Come on, come up on stage! Let's do Doga!" as Simon exclaims, "Here we go!" Manny and Robbie commence a synchronized dance, with Manny showcasing impressive flexibility, including balancing her leg behind her head, while Robbie remains calm and attentive. The black dog becomes animated, running across the glossy, reflective stage. Manny excitedly exclaims "Oh my god!" and instructs, "You gotta take your shoes off and your socks!" The judges react with visible astonishment; Simon covers his mouth, Amanda smiles broadly, and Alesha claps enthusiastically. Simon asks, "Did he wink?" to which David replies, "Oh, he did now," amidst audience laughter. Simon then jokes, "I think I'll let the little dogs carry on and we'll go back on the main stage," and later comments, "Wow! That's what you thought of the act, huh?" Manny continues her Doga instructions, "Okay, let's do the upward paw pose. All of them rise up, extend, extend," as the background music shifts to a calming, ethereal tune with female vocalizations. The segment concludes with the judges and audience applauding, and the stage lights flashing in celebration of the energetic performance and the bond between the performers and their dogs.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a woman in a black top, smiling and adjusting her hair, as the camera quickly pans to a wide shot of a talent show stage featuring a glossy floor, a large star backdrop, and dynamic blue and purple lighting. A man in a suit walks across the stage, followed by the woman in the black top and another man in a white shirt, who then take their seats at a judging panel with prominent red buttons. The judges are heard discussing a previous act, with a male voice asking, "Whose dog was it?" and a female voice stating, "we all have our dogs on this show." They proceed to vote, with one female judge giving "It's a no from me," while two others offer "yes" votes. A male judge then asks, "What do you think, Bert? Did you enjoy it?", to which a male judge, Bert, replies, "Yes, I did," leading to the announcement of "three yeses." The scene transitions to a woman with short blonde hair in a purple tank top and patterned leggings, performing a dance routine on stage while holding a small, fluffy white dog under bright blue spotlights, accompanied by upbeat, energetic pop music and enthusiastic audience clapping and cheering. The camera cuts back to the judging panel, where a woman with blue hair in a floral blazer is seen asking questions, and a man in a beige suit responds. The segment then introduces a new act, 'Dogs of Anarchy,' with a man in a military-style outfit, later identified as Alex, standing on stage with two dogs, a brown one named Princess Kira and a tan one named Einstein Brega, as confirmed by on-screen text and Alex's own Polish dialogue. The judges, including a woman in a red top and the man in the beige suit, react with surprise and amusement, while the audience continues to clap and cheer, their excited faces captured in close-ups. The judges engage Alex in a discussion in Polish, with the woman in the floral blazer asking, "Who did you come to us with?", and Alex responding, "with Princess Kira and with Einstein Brega." She further inquires, "And for example, if you want them to run quickly when you call?", prompting Alex to demonstrate by calling out, "Kira Brega." The judge then asks, "Alex, how old are you?", to which Alex replies, "24," eliciting surprised reactions from the judges, including a male judge exclaiming, "Damn! He could be my son," and the woman in the floral blazer continuing her line of questioning about his dogs.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a striking close-up of Princess Kira, a brown dog, baring her teeth in a snarl, as a judge with blue hair and a floral blazer asks in Polish, "Are these your only dogs?" The scene cuts to the judging panel, where the judge continues questioning, "And where exactly did you get these dogs, tell me?" The performer, a young man in a military-style outfit, stands on stage with Princess Kira and Einstein Brega, a tan dog, and responds in Polish with an earnest, emotional tone. He explains that Princess Kira was "most likely from an intervention," having been through a lot, biting people and being afraid of everything. He then recounts how Einstein Brega was "fished out of the water, as the only one who survived from his siblings." The performer shares his mission to prove that "dogs from adoption can also be taught a lot," detailing how he has helped other aggressive dogs find homes. As emotional, uplifting background music begins, he reveals his personal story: he was once a "rebellious teenager" with a "very weak psyche," but thanks to his dogs, he avoided "strange things like drugs." He recounts how he overcame his insecurities by taking the dogs out, opening up to people. Intimate moments of the performer petting the dogs, one lying calmly on a step and another being gently held, are interspersed with shots of the brightly lit stage and outdoor scenes with greenery, visually reinforcing the profound, transformative bond between the performer and his once-traumatized companions.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of a brown dog, Princess Kira, baring her teeth in a snarl, as a judge with blue hair and a floral blazer asks about the number of dogs the performer has. The scene cuts to the judging panel, where the judge continues questioning, and then to the performer, a young man in a military-style outfit, standing on stage with two dogs. He explains that Princess Kira was previously aggressive and had bitten people, while Einstein Brega was the sole survivor of a litter found in water. The performer shares his personal story, revealing that he was once a 'rebellious teenager' with poor mental health, but the dogs helped him avoid becoming a drug addict. He recounts how he opened up to people by taking the dogs out, overcoming his insecurities. The video includes intimate moments of the performer petting the dogs, one lying calmly on a step, and another being gently held. The setting shifts between the brightly lit stage with red curtains and warm lighting, and outdoor scenes with greenery and pink flowers, emphasizing the emotional bond between the performer and his dogs. The narrative is underscored by subtitles that highlight the dogs' past traumas and the transformative impact they had on the performer’s life.
